Mm, okay.
Is Kal'tsit back? I'll go get the door.
It's you...
You're the Tin Man who ate four big-ass hot dogs in one go!
Finally made it out of District 13.

I'll have to thank Director Jara for the new equipment. The drone's rotor is probably made of some kind of high-performance optical material, its camouflage ability has increased greatly, and the surveillance range has doubled...
Now what next?
This is my only clue... it doesn't matter where it leads me, as long as it brings me closer to the truth.
The Transmitter's response has been growing stronger ever since I approached District 9...
If I remember correctly, the park right here should be closed due to some government planning issues.
Irregular geometric shapes of silver float slowly in the air, accompanied by the buzz of a high-frequency hum.
It is another Transmitter, but it seems to be slightly different than the one in her hand, and Silence could not make out what the specific differences were.
An aged Caprinae in the middle of the room nods slightly, as if he had heard someone call him. The Transmitter draws a huge arc in the air as it lands on his shoulder.
Long time no see, Silence. You look well.
Silence pulls the excited Transmitter back into her arms, and slowly walks towards the other party.
...Professor.
Made it all the way here from Site 359? You're so much braver than you were before.
But... I never thought... the man I found... would be you.
I heard you helped Dorothy. Very good.
Ferdinand is too hungry for success, and Dorothy is an extremely obstinate woman. Things might not have ended as they did without you.
You...
I know you have many questions you want to ask.
But first, pass me that crystalline unit over there.
Silence picks up the experimental instrument next to her, walks over to Parvis and puts it down where his finger is pointing.

She connects the unit to the operating terminal's port, and Parvis is already seated in front of the monitor without even glancing at her as a slew of complex equations frantically calculate themselves on the display,
Only when Silence stands up does she realize that he had given the order as naturally as she had executed it.
I'm a busy man, but you've done very well to find me here. So I'll tell you whatever you want to know.
Are you all using Transmitter?
Indeed.
What exactly... is it being used for?
The Transmitter is extremely sensitive to micro-vibrations.

It can receive and encode nerve signals, and Dorothy hoped it would replace traditional Arts Units, becoming a brand-new medium that would allow everyone connected to it to be completely equal individuals.
And Ferdinand intends to use its 'superorganism' characteristics to manufacture super-equipment that can accurately deliver weapons across vast distances.
So you know everything that happened at Site 359?
Parvis does not answer.
Silence carefully observes her professor's expression. It is not as if he is not trying to avoid it or is bored by the question, but rather, it is not even worth answering at all.
So if you knew about their research all along, then... on whose side are you on? Dorothy's, Ferdinand's, or Control's?
Or is it as my colleagues in the break room once said, the Director of Structural is an old goat with a permanent smile on his face who always chooses whatever is most convenient for him?
I'm not on any particular side, Silence.
I helped them because both Ferdinand and Dorothy are hard workers, youths full of ideals and enthusiasm. The same goes for you.
Whoever I don't support, is due to their mediocre capacity as scientists. Me included.
We are unable to transcend this mediocre and feeble era.
What about you? What do you intend to do with the Transmitter?

That's enough, Silence. It's that I'm unwilling to answer you, but this question exceeds your level of understanding. I have much to do, and lack the luxury of time to tell you everything from the very beginning.
Though you may stay, you are one of my best students after all.
Soon, we shall have the most ideal environment for our experiment, one that meets all our material requirements and catalytic conditions... so now, we must prepare ourselves for it.
It will be the most disruptive experiment in history, and you will bear witness to the birth of true greatness.
It is neither short-sighted nor feeble, it will transcend every limit there is, and save us all from mediocre progress.
This is the real and rightful promise of science itself.
The Pioneers at Site 359, the workers at the Triton Factory... Professor, your research has hurt many innocent people.
Will it harm even more?
This is science, Silence. As scientists, we need only consider one thing and one thing alone.
And that is how to accomplish it.
That alone is more than enough to exhaust us.
Parvis is speaking very calmly. More precisely, he even sounds a little distracted.
The equation on the screen seems to have to do with the Transmitter. Parvis never turns to look at Silence. His eyes are fixated only on the equation.
Silence can tell very clearly what it is that she is feeling right this moment: Anger. She clenches her fist.
I see. No wonder I couldn't get in touch with Saria or Kal'tsit.
Dr. Doctor, I can task you with this matter all the same.
I heard about what happened from Kal'tsit.
It's been a long time. When last we met...
Uh, speaking of... Are you afraid of me because of the way I look? You've been staring for a bit.

opts: Are your organs made of tin, too? Or is there nothing in your belly?
What happens if your skin rusts? Does that count as a skin disease?
Do you get arthritis? Do you put WD-40 on your joints?

lines.x: You've changed, Doctor.
Back on topic. We don't have much time, and given your condition... There is not much catching up to do.
The Maylander Foundation wishes for Rhodes Island to help us stop the military... from assassinating the Vice President.
Doctor, you don't seem surprised.
We've just got this piece of intel ourselves. Tomorrow, Jackson will visit Rhine Lab, and that's when Blake will make his move.
No doubt Kal'tsit has already gone over the 'Horizon Arc Project' with you, so I won't touch on that.
Rhodes Island should be on the same side as the Maylander Foundation in this. It's a promise that Kal'tsit made to me, and it is also the prerequisite for your entry into Trimounts.
Assist the Maylander Foundation in stopping the military's absurd act of treason. You can see this as an 'order,' Doctor.

opts: Maylander should have enough agents to spare, no?
You aren't here to find the Vice President some bodyguards, are you?

lines.x: You're still as calm and sharp as ever. That matches the impression I had of you.
Right, the Maylander Foundation is already allocating our resources to ensure Vice President Jackson's safety, but that alone will not stop the military's plan.
One of the events after the press conference includes a visit to Rhine Lab. His entire trip that day will be broadcast live.

If his assassins and our agents come to blows before the public, it doesn't matter if the assassination itself is successful or not. Blake will still have a reason to declare martial law and take over Trimounts.
Just like District 13 right now.
Neither I nor Maylander likes it when the enemy has all their eggs in one basket.
Because that's always an omen of the collapse to come.

opts: You sure you don't want Kal'tsit's opinion first?
You're gonna leave this to me, just like that?

lines.x: This is an official request that Maylander is making to Rhodes Island. You are the Doctor of Rhodes Island. If Kal'tsit continues to have her faith in you, I have no reason to doubt your abilities.
Time's a wasting, Doctor. I hope you will cooperate with us well.

opts: Maybe I should try to get in touch with Kal'tsit.
Maybe I should try to get in touch with Saria.
Too bad Mechanist just left Trimounts.

lines.x: Doctor, let me and Rosmontis catch the hitman who's after the Vice President!
Kal'tsit's still looking for the entrance to that 'Arc-01' thing, and Saria's gone back to Rhine Lab to look for data. Doctor, we have to get on this right now!
Or are you still treating me like a kid, just like Saria and Silence?
Doctor, you've brought me on so many successful missions now. In two more years, I'll get to apply to become an elite operator. Oh, Rosmontis's already elite...
Anyway, if you're gonna lock me in the hotel room—

opts: I'm just wondering what kind of mission I should assign you, Operator Ifrit.

lines.x: Hey, watch where you are going!
You're gonna get hit by a car, spacing out like that!
Let me leave you with a little advice, Researcher Silence.
You may think helping Anthony is the right thing to do.
But that couldn't be further from the truth.
It's silly to think that Simon Co. was just the victim in their fight with HydeBro.
They were fighting because they were hellbent on bringing down the other side. It's just that old Smith didn't play the game as well as his opponent.
In other words, if Smith managed to make his move before HydeBro, it would've been the Hydes hunkering down in Bunkerhill right now instead of his family.
From that point of view, is Anthony really just a victim?
But...
What do you think, Anthony?
I can't deny it.
But there are no 'ifs' in this world. I know that.
I'm telling you all this because I want you to understand something—

If you want to do the right thing, you need to first be able to tell what's actually right. You also need to understand that doing the right thing doesn't necessarily mean you don't have a price to pay.
It's not just Energy and Originium Art. I can't tell you where this trail will lead and how many people it will end up dragging into the mess.
But you will no doubt come to see... a side of Rhine Lab that you have never known.
Be it the 'Diαbolic Crisis' or Site 359, you know that Rhine Lab is nowhere near as wonderful as the recruitment videos make it seem. Even then, it may still... disgust you.
If it comes down to it, you may use my permissions as the director of the Originium Art Section.
If you use my permissions to do anything else, I will turn a blind eye to that too.
Director Franks, I won't...
But Silence, remember this. Your mission is to recover the batch of Transmitters lost at Site 359 and nothing else.
If you run into any obstacles too difficult to overcome, you are free to give up any time you want. There are some things that, until you find your own answer, you have no power to fight.
This is science, Silence. As scientists, we need only consider one thing and one thing alone.
And that is how to accomplish it.
That alone is more than enough to exhaust us.
You're a lot more composed now, but your eyes are the same as back during Haydn 1...
Angry over that sick child, criticizing me for those minor issues during the procedures, and even blaming science itself.
But as I have so often told you, many times in the past...
Only science can change what needs to be changed, it can advance an entire age, but its progress lies in the hands of a limited few.
This is a natural paradox, Silence, and a paradox simply implies a cost.
I have already given you the clues you have been wanting.
But, Silence. What can you do? You don't even know where to start your inquiries.
After leaving Parvis's lab, Silence spends the next half hour walking aimlessly along the streets of District 9.
She had followed the Transmitter all the way here, but what should she be doing now? What could she even do?
Stop her own mentor? Find Control and beg for the truth from her?
He was right. How could she stop anyone if she doesn't even know where to begin?
She is angry, but anger itself holds no meaning whatsoever.
Holy shit!
Hey, you! Liberi chick! Out of the way!
At a perfectly regular intersection in Trimounts' District 9, a driver anxiously confirms the current time.
He needs to get to his destination within the next ten minutes, to block off a difficult client in time and allow the other party to sign the contract.
But in the next second, he feels as if he is suddenly on a roller coaster ride, as if invisible slopes had formed with the air, his car climbs, turns, accelerates and turns into a cannonball.
Water.
Drops of water suddenly appear and chain the car's wheels to a halt, and the out-of-control vehicle seems to almost graze Silence as it slides past her, scorching the tarmac with a shrill sound.
Half the road is completely drenched, as if it has only just rained.
A-Are you okay? Sorry, I have no idea what just happened...
Silence, Trimounts has a ton of cars on the road. You could get run over if you keep spacing out like that.
Director Muelsyse? Just now...
I'm just passing by. No need to thank me.
By the way, when did you piss off that psycho?
Knowing what she's capable of, she might have been more direct and messed with the air. Looks like it was just a prank she pulled while she was at it.
What are you talking about?
Never mind. Nothing that crazy woman does surprises me anymore.
Huh? Silence, when did you change into this new uniform?! The material looks great, and it fits you perfectly. You look like a completely different person. I almost couldn't tell it was you!
Speaking of which, I didn't recognize Iffy, either. She got new threads, and she's so much taller now!
Ifrit? What is Ifrit doing in Trimounts?
She's with Rhodes Island's Doctor.
The Doctor is here, too?
Huh? I thought you two were here together.
Muelsyse's surprised expression is very vivid, to the point that Silence immediately realizes what she is here for.

There is no doubt that Muelsyse is doing this out of kindness. The games that the higher-ups are playing are too dangerous, and she is trying to get this lowly researcher to stay away from Rhine Lab while this is going on.
Haydn 1 Laboratory, Site 359, Triton Factory...
Parvis, Dorothy, Jara, Muelsyse...
She has stumbled her way to this point. How many obstacles will she still have to face? All of a sudden, Silence is struck with a sense of helplessness that she has never felt before.
Where are Ifrit and the Doctor?
I was only going to have you make some trouble for Blake. Never did I expect you to follow the trail to the old goat...
The Elf saved you, but where will she take you now?
Oh, well. Just a little birdie. No need to worry about her.
Hurry! All the headlines will be the Vice President's visit to Rhine tomorrow. The editor-in-chief will have our heads if we don't get a good photo.
Wait, I can't find my press pass...
What? You can't go in without it!
Go find a good spot! I'll look for it.
So many people...
I couldn't even get to the café downstairs for breakfast.
A public visit by the Vice President, broadcasted live. Every newspaper is here. It's a wonder they didn't completely block the roads.
You see it, Rosmontis?
What?
First the factory, then Rhine Lab. Trimounts likes to block off a place when something happens.
Rhine Lab is different from the factory, isn't it? They blocked off some of the key roads leading in. Probably out of security concerns for the Vice President's convoy.
Which causes other streets to fill up.
Did they think people wouldn't see if they blocked it off? That's dumb.
...Mm-hm.
Doctor, what are you looking at?
Sunny all day long! The hotel TV had the weather report on when were leaving. Guess you didn't see it.
Anyway, it's not the rainy season yet.
Another sunny day.

The urban planning of Trimounts is intricate, the gaps between the staggered skyscrapers allowing sunlight to pass through, for the sun to paint each street with warm light evenly in these early days of winter.
You look up to see a dark cloud in the distance, floating nonchalantly in the sky. The wind is blowing toward the cloud, and it looks to be moving away from the city.
But it never disappears.
You also see the busy streets, the parked cars, a flash of light up high in the distance, and well-disguised 'passersby'...
You keep a poker face while urging the two girls on.
Did you find something, Doctor?
Don't worry, Doctor, we'll protect you.
I know, I know. You've said it a dozen times before we left.
I promised to listen to you, and I keep my promises.
I think we're there.
Dr. Doctor. We meet again.
As usual, I'm the one waiting.
Ah, well, I got here before you. I got myself ready as soon as your message came in.

opts: This is your company, after all...

lines.x: Well, this is what you asked for... plans for the Rhine Lab building, and visitor's passes for you and your young assistants.
I called in a few favors to get you clearance to most of the building and data. Today only.
There are lots of hidden spaces in the building, both above and below ground. My tip is to have someone focus on the underground.
I have to accompany Mr. Vice President on his Rhine tour, of course.
Kristen's not here, and we're missing a few section heads. Can't have anyone else calling out.
I'll give you a big, personal thank you when we're all done.

opts: You may be on your own, Ifrit.

lines.x: I read your resignation letter, Bieber. Why so sudden?
I'm sorry, Director Jara.
I discovered something yesterday.
Go on.
Energy's wing has 38 ceiling lamps and 21 vents.
The carpet beneath our feet looks the same at first glance, but a closer look reveals that there are actually three versions, subtly different in pattern and fringes.
I didn't know you were planning to go into interior design, Bieber.
You would've done the same if all the projects you're involved in are up in the air, and the only work you have to do each day is to re-read the report that you wrote the day before.
I understand. It's hard work, keeping your guys calm while pretending that everything is business as usual.
I could live with hard work, Director.

The labs have always been relatively independent of one another, and research is modular. Everybody goes about their own business, even if something happens to Site 359, even if Ferdinand quits unexpectedly.

At the lower levels, people don't know much and believe this is no different from any number of 'research accidents' that have happened before, the kind that every tech company deals with every now and then.
But I know this one is different...

Control has virtually vanished without a trace, and Ferdinand suddenly resigned. Every day is like sitting on a fire. There's been no formal change of leadership in Energy, no one to tell me what happened, or what I should do.
Now the Vice President is touring Rhine. It makes my stomach seize up when I walk into the lab and see the excitement in everybody's eyes.
So you talked to Volvort Kochinski's headhunter... Sonya, right?
I... yes.
I'm not reprimanding you, Bieber.
Your position at Rhine Lab allows you to see more than the average employee, but not the full picture. It's normal to feel anxious.
The responsibility falls on me. I asked you to keep things calm in the Energy Section, but neglected your own emotional needs.
I-It's not your fault. Just weakness on my part...
Why do you think the Vice President is making this sudden visit?
I... I don't know. But he'll find out that we're...
The press conference that he held a few days ago has to do with us.
...Really?
That's all I can tell you for now.
How about this? Take one month off. Paid leave. Maybe it'll be enough for you to clear your head and return to your post.
If you still insist on resigning after that, I won't stop you.
Rhine Lab grew from a single lab to ten sections, running more than a hundred large-scale projects simultaneously. It has seen bigger storms.
It has survived them all.
Just give it a little thought.
Sorry for the trouble, Sonya.
The staff need a way to vent in times like this.
Bieber should be the last one. If he's okay, the Energy Section is okay for now.
Yeah, catch you for lunch or dinner one of these days. Give Tin Man my thanks.
*sigh*...
Come in.
How many does that make this week?
Not your problem.
Just don't send them to Business if you're reassigning them. I don't need flighty idiots like that.
They're cornerstones of this company, Justin, just as you are.
And I may be old, but I'm not senile enough to assign a PhD in Energy Mechanics to be one of your salesmen.
The Vice President is almost here. I doubt you have the time to fool around in my office.
Just giving you a heads-up. I'm seeing a few new investors today, and I have a number of important remote meetings to attend.
Since the Vice President is here to inspect research, I'll excuse myself.
None of the phones in Business stopped ringing the day the Vice President announced his visit to Rhine. I had to get someone to add two dedicated hotlines.
It's a clear signal. The world of Trimountian finance...
No, not just Trimounts, not just finance. Every Columbian who even has a passing interest in investing has their eyes on us.
That Bieber has a lot of stock options, doesn't he? I say you just accept his resignation, then hire him back at a lower salary when the company's stock soars and he begs for it.
Rhine Lab is not a stock market toy, Justin.
Now that is hurtful.
Mayor Benjamin, Councilman Chris, Deputy Phil, Commander Colette, even the Chief Justice... that's just a few of the people I'm talking to today.
Could Kristen have kept playing her hide-and-seek game with Blake if I hadn't continued to sweet-talk all these friends?
You utilized a lot of resources...
Just doing my job.
And one correction... I'm not a cornerstone of Rhine Lab.
A cornerstone exists for other people to build upon.
I don't have the same loyalty to Kristen as you do. I chose her and Rhine.
I helped her because I wanted to take control of my own fate, of everyone's fates.
This isn't a game.
News from Maylander?
Yes, sir. They have personnel deployed around the area to ensure your safety.
Good. How much further?
Two more blocks until Rhine Lab headquarters.
Canceling today's tour is always an option, sir, if you're concerned.
If the Maylander Foundation can't be trusted, then canceling the tour won't be enough to protect me.
I can't very well skip class to see the mining platforms chase the sun because I'm scared of exams, like I did when I was young.
Not when I'm the Vice President of the Columbia Union.
You should get some rest while you can, sir. I doubt there will be many more chances today.
Do you remember how I described Trimounts at yesterday's banquet?
'The future of Columbia.' You said you love this youthful city, just as you love this youthful nation.
As a matter of fact, I don't like this place at all.
There are no burdenbeasts, no clouds of dust kicked up by their hooves, no duels for some 'dumb' reason, no pioneering spirit.
Only white-collar workers and their coffee, high-rise buildings that split the sky, and... *sniff* air so clean it makes your stomach squirm.
I believe the Trimountian circulation system employs—
Don't, Pavard. I'll be hearing far too many explanations like that over the next few hours.
You don't like dealing with scientists?
I won't go that far.
They're simple. They can go on and on about their products, blueprints or ideals, sometimes outlandish, sometimes crazy, mostly incomprehensible...
All I need to do is smile, nod, and shake their hands when they're done, giving them a few words of encouragement.
Much simpler than dealing with the old folks of the special district.
You represent the state. The people like to see Columbia support science. It's good for your image, and your odds in the next election.
The vice presidential election, Columbia's biggest political event... but I'm thinking that perhaps I shouldn't spend too much time and effort on it.
You've achieved a lot in the past several years. The polls indicate you enjoy a high level of support...
The representatives who support you have also been in contact with that influential Vulpo director of Rhine Lab. It's not easy to gain the support of big tech. You have a head start.
No, I meant in comparison with Mark Max, the President ever guiding Columbia to her promised future.
All that national attention surrounding the vice presidential elections just adds to the embarrassment.
What is this screen showing?
The cargo passageway behind Rhine Lab.
A little to the left. Zoom in... good, now there shouldn't be any dead zones.
The soldier is nervous as he operates the equipment. The Colonel has been standing behind him for some time now, his brow furrowed.
The five blocks around Rhine Lab headquarters have all been placed under surveillance.
Many figures are moving on the screens. Key areas have been blocked off, but there continues to be a considerable crowd nearby.
Of course, the crowd also provides good cover for their operations.
The soldier scans each monitor, not daring to even blink.
What's the situation on the ground?
All undercover and support units are in position.
Good.
Where is Ferdinand?
He's on the phone.
The phone?
Yes. Should I summon him?
Never mind, no need to tell him.
What about the colleagues in the Defense Section that he talked about?
We've 'taken over.'
Good.
A shadow flashes across the display on the bottom-left, before appearing on the other screens. It is the Columbia displayed on top of a car.
Several black vehicles maintain equal separation with it, both in front and behind, with machine-like discipline.
Begin.
The Vice President's convoy is in sight. Begin.
Have a nice tour, Jackson.
Mr. Vice President! There have been reports that Triton factory explosion in District 13 was linked to Rhine Lab.
Is your visit to Rhine Lab at this time intended to dispel such speculation?
Excuse me.
District 13 is still under lockdown. Did you authorize the army's actions, and how long will the lockdown remain in effect?
Will you be meeting with Control Wright?
Excuse me... Mr. Vice President, about your schedule in Trimounts...
Patience, please.
There will be a press conference after the Vice President has concluded his tour.
We have set aside a space to accommodate members of the press. Please, follow me, and refrain from disturbing other staff members in their work.
There's no one else?
Damn!
Shut him up.
Mmph—
Look at that, hiding weapons in recording equipment. Search him and make sure he doesn't have anything else on him.
There's a discrepancy in the documents... find a way to check the credentials of all the reporters in the press area. Be discreet.
I got this one. No commotion.
Lost contact with A1, A2 A3...
Have all our locations been compromised?!
HQ, HQ, need immediate instruction on whether to continue operation—
Grgh...
That's the 9th one.
If you're used to dealing with terrorists, you know a thing or two about planning a terror attack.
Report.
All potential vantage points within sniping range of Rhine Lab have been secured.
We have neutralized nine snipers and eleven disguised assassins.
Continue to monitor the area around Rhine Lab. Roads, vehicles, pedestrians... leave no stone unturned.
Roger.
The Vice President has entered the Rhine building. It will be difficult for us to deploy directly inside the building. Do you intend to be on scene personally?
I got a message from a colleague. There's somewhere else that I need to be.
Share intel with Dr. Doctor of Rhodes Island. Follow any orders issued by the Doctor.
Acknowledged.
This one makes seven.
Just mass-produced power armor, Doctor.
Don't worry, it won't take too much effort.
The serial numbers match the ones that the Defense Section lost, but five are still unaccounted for.
Should we keep looking? We need to eliminate them before they leave the secret passage, right?
They'll cause chaos if they get out, even if they can't get to the Vice President.
How did you think of using the access permissions granted by Muelsyse to check the Defense Section's equipment log?

opts: Just going down the list of possible options. I didn't expect to strike gold on the first try.
I didn't think the assassins would throw acid at the Vice President disguised as researchers.

lines.x: I see.

opts: Something feels off. These mechs seem cautious in their movements.
It doesn't seem so much an assassination attempt as...

lines.x: Movement, Doctor.
No. Who could be in a secret passage at this hour...?
Doctor, Operator Rosmontis... what are you doing here?
I see... sorry, Doctor, I've been maintaining radio silence because I had infiltrated Rhine to retrieve data for Kal'tsit.
As for Kal'tsit's whereabouts, I have no idea either.
The remaining power armor...
I destroyed five before running into you and the Doctor. That makes a total of twelve, which matches the number that the Defense Section lost.
And so, I went to find someone.
I'm guessing the feeling of being dragged out of a suit of power armor is not something you want to experience again, Reggie.
What orders did Ferdinand give you?
He only wanted to borrow a few suits of power armor.
Ferdinand can't be trusted.
What about you?
How do I trust someone who repeatedly ignores the rules that she set, showing up at Rhine whenever she wants, who abandoned all her responsibilities?
He took over Defense when you left. He paid our wages for the past few years.
He remembers our names, our hobbies, even gives us gifts, every once in a while.
Ferdinand may have been an asshole.
But I have to say, Director Saria—
That asshole paid our bills.
The Vice President is still inside Rhine. You know the consequences if something happens.
We must check everything that Ferdinand may have touched.
Just the ten suits of power armor. You have my word.
I destroyed seven, Saria destroyed five...
That's impossible!
Ferdinand only borrowed ten. I watched him enter the commands myself!

opts: What kind of man is Ferdinand, Saria?

lines.x: Proud, arrogant, thinks himself better than everyone else.
But if there's one thing that sets him above other people...
It's that he actually puts his boasts into action.

opts: I don't think those ten mechs are for assassinating the Vice President.

lines.x: What?

opts: As for the two extra ones...
Looks like he and the army are on guard against each other.
Ferdinand doesn't want the Vice President assassinated at Rhine Lab.

lines.x: I feel like I'm a kid in a classroom, having a science lesson for the first time. It's no hyperbole at all to say that 'science is the future of Columbia.'
I regret not being able to stay longer and hear about your other projects.
You are always welcome at Rhine Lab, Mr. Jackson.
Thank you, Director Jara. I hope my visit didn't cause too much inconvenience.
Kind of you to say so. It was a pleasure to be your tour guide today.
Having the famous Marianne Blake as a tour guide alone makes this trip to Trimounts worthwhile.
Between the two of us, I'm a pretty big fan of yours.
I have an autographed poster of your debut film on the office wall.
Really?
A present from my wife when I was elected.
Kindly make a note to follow up with the special district government about Rhine Lab's application for a new research facility.
Will do, sir.
I guess that will be it for today, Miss Jara.
I wish you a pleasant stay at Trimounts.
Thank you.
May the sun shine on the next day, as it has today.
Come on, mash that photo button!
Mr. Vice President! What did you think about your trip to Rhine today?
Ask your questions in order, please! We have ten minutes, there's no rush.
You heard me right. I'm clapping.
'Vice President's Visit Bolsters Rhine Lab’s Claim as Industry Leader'... the headlines write themselves.
The tour concluded without incident. The Vice President will leave Rhine Lab in ten minutes.
...Doctor.
I got the map you sent, Doctor.
So there's a secret passage that leads right to the underground structure, but it's not in the plans that Muelsyse gave you?
Leave it to me!
I've been sitting here twiddling my thumbs while you and Rosmontis have fun with the power armor up there. Didn't even see a shadow.
Saria is coming for me?
Then I have to go to the underground structure too. The Vice President is leaving in a few minutes, right? We can't take any risks.
(Whisper) Ifrit moving out, Doctor.
I'm back.
How are things up there?
Not sure. I didn't quite make it up to the surface, but there was no commotion.
I'm guessing the attack failed.
What about us?
We activated the jammers and aren't supporting any other units... I don't think anyone is coming here. Ferdinand's route was very well-concealed.
Let's carry out the plan. It's almost time.
The explosives?
Set up while you were scouting... two tubes of advanced plastic explosives, enough to breach the containment structure.
You know, I thought Ferdinand wanted to come back to Rhine Lab. But this is the liquified Originium pipeline used by his Energy Section.
The Colonel says he's stuck in the same boat as us. He has no choice.
Don't really see the point of blowing this up now, though...
The Vice President should have left Rhine Lab by now. What good will blowing up the Originium pipes in the underground structure do?
The Colonel has his plans. All we need to do is follow orders.
Who goes there?
A... kid?
Looks like the Doctor's intel was good.
Hey, you, I don't know why you're hiding here, but I think you oughta surrender now.
We lost contact with all units?
Yes, sir. Sudden comms failure.
The Vice President and his retinue, the press... no sign that anything is amiss. Looks like our guys were neutralized before they could move.
Reports indicate that our operations within Rhine are also being disrupted by the sudden appearance of some power armor.
Hmph.
We're also losing signal from the cameras on the streets.
We're losing control of the scene, sir.
So, our best laid plans are like a sieve to the intelligence network of the Maylander Foundation.
It's not surprising that they're able to respond, given how hastily this assassination plan was hatched.
Should we pull out, sir?
They're bound to trace the trail back here, sooner or later.
No hurry...
Mr. Vice President said he would like to 'have a nice chat with top researchers.' I intend to oblige him.
Situation at C1 and C2?
Still in position. Signal stable.
Activate the remote control on the top-right of the panel, Riker.
Yes, sir.
The remote control Blake referred to is a mundane mechanical button, fitted with an old-fashioned safety lock.
The soldier enters the password, and the cover pops open, revealing the red button.
What is this, sir?
Remote control for the explosives that will blow up the containment around the liquified Originium pipes.
Look at the screen. According to the routes that Ferdinand provided, the Energy Section has several liquified Originium pipelines running through the west wing of Rhine Lab.
Rhine Lab Engineering does a thorough job.
In addition to standard safety measures, there are also underground containment structures that prevent chain reactions in the event of a leak.
The original plan was to cause a leak with an explosion, creating a chance for operatives on the ground to approach Jackson.
By chance, however, Jackson's convoy will cross over one of these pipelines on the way back.
You're Rhine Lab staff?
I heard the Defense Section has a lot of bad-tempered people, but I'm surprised they would hire a kid so young.
I told you to surrender. I didn't say you could talk.
I'll try to short stop of turning you to ashes. The Doctor and Saria may want to question you, after all.
Wait, is that a flamethrower?
Stop! There are liquified Originium pipes all around us!
So that's what's going on...
The flaming-red Savra girl makes her decision in a split second. She re-engages the flamethrower's safety lock, and swings it hard at the one on the left.
Then she charges towards the one on the right, and catches the side of his face with her fist.
In the time it takes for a flame to flicker, Ifrit is back on her two feet again, dusting off her palms.
Grgh...
I think my nose is broken...
Run, kid...
What?
What's that sound?
Damn, he started the countdown...
Less than one minute left—
Can C1 and C2 make it back?
Their contributions will be remembered, as well all others who give their lives this day.
But Colonel... it's an entire convoy. Five cars, a dozen people in the retinue, possibly even Rhine staff members and reporters, and other passersby...
You have your orders, Riker.
But I...
I'll hit the switch myself. That's my job. Your job is to report as soon as you get a signal from C1 and C2.
I won't defend myself if I stand trial for this someday.
So I'm not going to waste my time explaining myself to my soldiers now.
Yes, sir.
We have the signal!
The Vice President's convoy is reaching the target area on the west side of Rhine Lab.
Colonel?
Blake places his hand on the cold mechanical button. A chill runs up his finger, and he pauses for a couple of seconds.
The soldier glances at his superior officer, whose brow is furrowed behind his sunglasses.
The soldier realizes that his commander is even more anxious than he is.
(Whisper) Damn it all to hell!
Blake presses the button.
There is no sound, only an explosion on some of the screens.
On the tiny screen, the collapsing streets were like foam boards underneath bubbling flame. Then the screen goes black, the camera destroyed by flying debris.
There is no sound, but Blake and the soldier could hear the deafening boom in their heads.
The surface should be close, but I can't see the path with all these stone slabs in the way...
Hey, are you two okay?
Oh no, a chain explosion.
Ifrit?!
Ifrit hears a familiar voice through the echoes of the explosion and the falling rubble.
Saria? I'm here!
I hear you... hang in there!
I have a lock!
Ifrit can hear Saria's voice shaking. Something touches her fingertips in the darkness. Calcium crystals? Saria's Originium Arts.
Like a stream of water, the crystals fill the gaps in the rubble, then expand, releasing a sickening crack—
Saria has forced an opening in the collapsed structure.
A hand reaches down from above. Ifrit grabs it and feels it pull her up, while the calcium tunnel shatters behind her.
Don't move.
I'm fine, just a little dusty.
Your face... were you crying?
I-I wasn't crying! Just a little anxious, that's all.
Alright.
Your hands are dusty too, Saria. You're making my face even dirtier.
Oh, sorry.
And these two?
They were the ones who planted the bomb that blew up the liquified Originium pipelines, so I brought them out.
But I couldn't stop the explosion...
You did well.
You really did.
What is it, Ifrit?
Silence? What are you doing here?
...Silence.
Why are you here?
Mr. Vice President, Mr. Pavard, it's safe to go now.
The convoy?
Just a couple of mechs blown up. No need to worry.
This car has been modified by the Engineering Section. It may not look much, but you can trust it.
I hope you brought your driver's license, Mr. Pavard. We'll take a special passage on the east side. We've planned a new route for you.
The rest of your retinue will return in small groups once the dust has settled.
Was the explosion expected?
No, we wouldn't have allowed an explosion of this size to occur if we knew about it in advance.
But Rhine Lab and the Maylander Foundation's work isn't done until you're back safely at your residence... the enemy has more than one plan, and we have more than one contingency.
Very good.
I just don't want to see 'Another Triton?' in the headlines tomorrow.
There's no need to worry, we already have a plan in place for the press.
Once again, I wish you a pleasant stay in Trimounts.
Thank you.
Silence! Finally! Ever since I entered the city I've been wondering when I would get to see you...
Um... S-Silence?
Ifrit, come over here.

Don't worry, Silence. I'm all good here, good enough to knock out a row of bad guys while carrying you in one arm and running laps around them! If you don't believe me, I'll even carry Saria with my other arm just to prove it! Ha... haha...
Stop speaking for a bit, you'll affect the drone readings.
Oh... okay.
Silence lowers her head to examine Ifrit, just like she used to do, with the same meticulous and gentle manner as ever, but Ifrit still feels very nervous.
She has never been scared of Silence getting angry at her.
One of the first things she learned growing up was that Silence's anger stemmed from a concern for her.
She knows that after Silence is angry, she helps her take care of the things that were doing her harm, and helps her chase off the things that were scaring her.
It was not real anger.
But Ifrit is scared of this silent Silence.
When Silence does not show an ounce of anger, and rather, is quieter than usual, she realizes—
Something must have hit Silence's limit.
The skies in Trimounts are always gloomy and grey, and the storm clouds are as big as monsters. No wonder you used to always walk into my room with your hair all drenched.
Oh, and 'hot dogs,' do you like 'em? The Doctor bought some for me and Rosmontis, and I wanna try one with strawberry-flavored syrup next time.
Silence...
I decided to come here myself. I bothered Dr. Kal'tsit about it for a long time, and also wrote the Doctor a super long letter.
I completed all my courses and trainings in the last few years, I can do a whole lot of missions, just like you and Saria...
Blood Originium-Crystal Density has slightly increased.
What are the estimated rates of increase for that and for Cell-Fusion Assimilation?
For now, they're within controllable ranges.
We won't know the rest of the results until Ifrit returns to the landship for a comprehensive examination.
You want me to return to the landship... you guys still don't trust me...
No, Ifrit. I trust you.
But...
There shouldn't be a need for any 'buts' here with Saria around!
Four years ago, it was in this very city, and under the very same signboard.
I almost lost you forever. Back then, she was at your side too, no different than right now.
But I lived, and I've been living good! Saria protected me, so this time I can protect you and everyone with her!
Ifrit, listen to Silence. She's your doctor, and you should trust her the most.
But I trust you too! I trust the Doctor, and Rosmontis too, there's no difference in the amount of trust I have in you guys.
Saria, I saw you the other day. You were with that mysterious man in a trenchcoat, at Triton Factory in District 13. And on the very same day, something fell from the sky.
So you were there too.
Just another Rhine-related secret experiment, right?
Of course you won't answer. Because... this is you trying to 'protect' me and Ifrit.
Just ten minutes ago, Ifrit stopped an assassination attempt on the Vice President.
What?

I'm not trying to conceal information from you, Silence, but what I have on hand is also very limited. There are simply too many different factions involved in this, and all I can do is try my best to prevent the situation from deteriorating even further.
'We must put this back on the right track'—I've heard that line from Director Saria too many times.
You were always in control of the situation back at Rhine Lab, but those horrifying experiments kept popping up, one after another.

There will always be someone hurt by the ambitions of another, and these wounds won't simply be healed by criminal charges and trials... Not to mention, a trial that should never have happened in the first place.
I can't help but wonder... when you people finally extinguish this fire, just how many will be left lying in the ruins, breathing their last breaths just like Ifrit was?
You perceive the situation more thoroughly and clearly than I can.

But you still choose out of habit, to help them 'control.' You hope to minimize any damage, and preferably, shrink it until it becomes unnoticeable, rather than put a stop to the immorality at its source.
I can't deny that.
Saria... I've been waiting years to ask you a question.
Your inability to make a genuine stand against Rhine, is it because deep in your bones, you've always been the same as them...? The same as Control?
Sniper!
Silence, get down! Ifrit—
What—
S... Saria...?
White calcium crystals float before her eyes.
They had briefly protected her and Ifrit just moments ago, but now they disintegrate silently as the caster falls.
Confirmed hit on the target!
Great.
This high-powered energy weapon is capable of blasting a hole open through an armored vehicle, and yet it wasn't able to penetrate her Arts.
This damned Vouivre and her Arts are tough as shit, harder than power armor.
You mean to say... There's a possibility she might survive?
Heh, 'possibility.' I'm so fed up with that word. All those researchers tossing it around.
What I want is results, definite results.
Then what happened with the Vice President...
Maylander won't give us a second chance.
The op team is still on standby. Should we order them to withdraw?
No need. Give them something else to do.
Just like how we dealt with that eyesore of a Vouivre, don't let anyone causing chaos in Rhine get off scot-free.
It's time for these idiots who think they can do whatever they want with just a few companies under their belts to wake up—
No one can challenge the Columbian Army's authority on Columbian soil and get away with it.
Move!
Blake, I didn't give you Rhine's contacts for you to drag it into the gutter!
Which is why you've used your channels within Rhine.
The guilty are often the first to complain, Ferdinand.
Are you aware of our current situation?
Someone disrupted the intel, and the detonator went off too early.
And now, reporters have already spun the story that this has nothing to do with Jackson at all.
What did you say?
I said, the assassination failed.
Are you satisfied?
Come on, Ferdinand. Put away whatever weapon you're hiding.
Do you really think you can even leave a scratch on me?
Relax, I don't blame you.
Blake pats Ferdinand's cheek twice.
Ferdinand expected Blake's eyes to be filled with a murderous intent, but to his surprise, all he sees is a smile.
Carrying out an assassination within Rhine given our current intel would have been extremely difficult, even without your meddling.
And even now, that company's all that's on your mind. Loyalty to your own desires isn't necessarily a bad thing, though.
I'm serious, Ferdinand. It's an admirable trait.
You're fit to be a soldier.
But now that we've failed—
What of it? This is just the beginning.
It's a good thing that Maylander has us pinned down. Do you know just how many of my superiors have been waiting ages to strike out against them?
Before the situation spirals out of control, our losses will only win me more support.
And if things really spiral—
Trust me, you don't want to know how things will end for me.
Of course, I can't guarantee how they'll end for you either.
I'll stick to our agreement, to help you find Kristen.
We can still turn the tables once we have her in our grasp.
That goes for the both of us... and Rhine too.
I'll go meet with Jara.
Sure... by all means.
Let's see if it's luck... or fate, that will catch up to us first.
Squad A, search that side.
Squad B, conduct your search over here.
Our targets are a hooded figure with no weapon, and a Feline girl able to use highly lethal Arts.
Doctor, my terminal lit up.
That means... it's time for me and Ifrit to meet up, but I can't sense her presence.

opts: Rhine should be safe for now.

lines.x: This place... isn't safe at all.
There are too many laboratories here. They all have the same white walls, just like a maze.
Ifrit hates this kind of place the most. Whenever she talks about those memories, her breaths become shorter and her hands get cooler than usual.
I want to go find her. I don't want to see my friend get hurt.
These walls aren't so tough. Just give me the order and I can get you out of here easily.
Thank you very much for choosing not to smash up our walls. We imported the material from Kazimierz. They're very expensive.
There's no need to be so nervous, little girl. As you can clearly see, I'm only a businessman. Nothing but a fountain pen and a wallet in my pocket.

opts: I didn't expect to see you here, Director Fitzroy.

lines.x: Haha, sure enough. My next meeting just happens to be around the corner, so I figured I would greet our guests on my way over.
...Areas C and D have been searched.
There's a ton of conference rooms in front, prepare to check them one by one.
Enemies in the corridors, and a lot of them too.
Give me the order, Doctor.
A hero working behind the scenes to prevent a terrible conspiracy, but soon to become the target of a clandestine arrest.
In Columbia, these sorts of stories are common enough, and such a script wouldn't fetch a good price in Wrankwood.

At the end of the day, is it a party being obstructed by the establishment and wanting to punish them for it, or the other party dispatching a commission to let this yet-to-be-exposed deal sink into the abyss—
What do you think?

opts: Seems like there's no need for guesses here.
Surely you showed up here at this moment for more than a greeting?

lines.x: Oops, would you just look at the time! They're waiting for me to start our meeting.
I hope I haven't disrupted your plans too much just by stalling you a bit?
Doctor, please stand back. I'm going to begin attacking.
My friends call me Justin Jr.

opts: The military seeing us together won't be good for you.

lines.x: What if I told them that the young and beautiful Feline lady by your side has something to do with a top-secret military experiment that should rightfully be covered in dust and long unused?

opts: Is that a threat from an enemy?
Is that a reminder from a friend?

lines.x: The choice lies with you.
Do remind me, just how many of our excellent employees have you already poached from Rhine Lab? Was it eight, or ten?
Jara has been thinking about getting acquainted with whoever runs your HR.
Since Rhodes Island and Rhine Lab have been cooperating so closely—the next time we meet, I might even have a contract for you with an offer you can't refuse.

opts: Rhodes Island thanks you for your interest.

lines.x: Alright, I won't bother you and Miss Feline here any longer. By the way, Dr. Doctor, after you get back, please do ask Muelsyse a question for me—
When she goes out to eat, what's her preferred cuisine?
Only one conference room left. The target is most likely inside.
Prepare to breach.
...It's empty?
Did you hear a sound just now?
What sound?
The sound of water. Ah, how wonderful it is.
It's... a lively energy, strong enough to fight even fate itself.
Saria... Saria!
She's not responding... no way...
Her head's suffered a serious impact, and though her vital signs are there, she's still in critical condition. She needs emergency treatment.
Ifrit, help me lift her arms... ugh, so heavy...
Don't even think about hurting Saria again!
Silence, I can burn a way out for us, and we can go try and meet up with the Doctor.
No. The people watching us are definitely also after Rhodes Island.
Yes, the military is already inside Rhine, and your friends might not be able to protect themselves.
Director Jara...?!
Come with me, kids.
I can't just sit by and watch the other founder of Rhine Lab fall here.
Mr. Vice President, Congress called back. It's regarding the bill to dismantle those military bases, they're ready to move the hearing up.
In addition, the Justice who reached out to us earlier has hinted that she's willing to cooperate with us to overturn the previous ruling.
The military is going to pay for their recklessness. We have an optimistic estimate that Colonel Blake will be out of Trimounts within the next twelve hours.
Everything is waiting for your decision, sir.
A call should be coming in, Pavard.
Yes, this is the Vice President's temporary office... Yes, he's right next to me... Yes...
Mr. Vice President, it's...
Hand it over, I know who's looking for me.
...Alright.
I understand. I feel the same. I'm not about to let everyone down at this critical juncture.
Mr. Vice President, about the bill...
Shelve it. There's no need to put this matter on the agenda for the next six months.
Is that... an order from the Maylander Foundation?
Pavard, how many casualties were there in that mess from your report?
We eliminated thirty military operatives.
And how much money do we spend on training these soldiers every year?
Three million to train a combat-ready man to DOD standards, sir.
So we basically just lost 90 million in cash and 30 outstanding talents.
...That's right.
So, there's no need to ask whose orders it is. It's a decision that's in the best interests of Columbia.
Go get me a meeting with Blake. It's time for us and the DOD to work together.
Understood.
I'll always remember the day the election results came in, when I thought smugly to myself that I could finally sit behind the desk of my dreams—and I received the biggest shock of my life.
Haha. Back then, I almost thought my country was caught up in a huge conspiracy.
But I've come to realize that's not what's happening.
Columbia isn't wrapped up in any conspiracy, and Maylander isn't the shadow government I thought it was.
It's just silly things happening in an orderly way, that's what makes it seem like we've been making real progress.
But I'm still just a regular guy. Every now and then, something crazy pops into my head.
If I really was caught in that explosion, then... would I be remembered as a martyr for Columbia? Or just tossed in the dustbin of history as some incompetent politician?
What... do you think?
The Vice President looks around the empty office, as if waiting for the omnipresent air to answer his question.
Then, the man standing so close to the apex of Columbian power sighs.
The military won't be bothering you any longer, so why the long face?
...Ho'olheyak, you are a difficult woman to reach.
Sorry about that. My boss, who I've not seen in a long time, has popped on over to Trimounts. I needed some time to compile my reports.
I'm afraid the Tin Man has forgot to send me any copies.
And that's why I've come to answer your question personally. As long as it's something I know, I'm happy to tell all.
Alright, you're the primary representative for the Maylander Foundation's Rhine Lab desk. So, tell me—
Where exactly is the main instigator of all this malarkey? Where is Kristen Wright right now?
She's in a place that not even the finest of Columbia's commandos are able to reach, a place never documented by the Maylander Foundation.
There's nowhere in Columbia like that.
It's not on any map of the country, at least for now. Even though the location might overlap with our territory, it is truly out of our line of sight.
So you mean...
Mr. Vice President, you are no doubt familiar with 'Project Exploration', an archaeological undertaking spanning multiple years led by the Maylander Historical Association... or rather, our Tin Man.
Those ancient ruins buried under the vast wasteland.
Exactly. Coincidentally, we also came across this information during a recent raid operation on Rhine-related personnel.
Exploration records?
These records are currently in the hands of a former executive of Rhine Lab, a close friend of Kristen Wright's... as well as an offshore company with an existing criminal record in Columbia.
And it is the most coincidental thing, that this company called Rhodes Island has a working relationship with my dear leader, the Tin Man.
...A water purification and circulation system.
You could also call it a 'sewage drain.'
It used to have another name, 'Locus of Sorcery.' Once the most primitive weapons of witchcraft from the Teekaz.
So that's its actual full name. To think that it was installed on the Pioneer Team's nomadic temporary housing.
Though it's certain that the device's core components have already long been lost.
All that is left are the outermost pipes, and you people... had a reasonable idea of how to recycle it.
I will take that as a compliment on behalf of the Columbians, Madame.
This country has gone through hard times. During the Great Expansion when supplies were scarce, every object was classified into only one of two qualities: useful or useless.
This may be a medium of some magnificent memories in your eyes, but to the ordinary people living in Columbia, they are useful pipes that will not be rusting anytime soon.
After all, no matter how old an object is, or how great an era it has seen, those unable to be comprehended by people of the current era will be no different from scrap.
Our own levels of understanding are but restricted by our own time and space. You and I are not exempt either... and the same goes for Kristen Wright.
She couldn't have drawn her power from a Teekaz-like legacy, and whatever outside force she has borrowed power from is much more powerful than that.
There are still thirty-two technologies waiting to be ruled out. Most of these are not on the Maylander Historical Association's exploration records.
I presume you won't be needing my company for whatever is coming next.
Sure enough. But this will not affect our partnership with Maylander.
Yes, indeed. This has always been your way of going about things.

You're hoping that Kristen will not get hold of that technology... but you also hope that Columbia won't be able to prematurely pocket that fortune for themselves. Still the same as always... selfless and neutral.
I have one more thing to ask of you.
We can discuss that first, Madame.
Dr. Doctor... and those two girls.
As you've said, there are some roads that only I can take. I'd like to trouble you to take care of them when it's inconvenient for me to do so.
Oh, I've misjudged you. You have changed after all, Madame. You're more humane than you once were.
No one, or thing is forever unchangeable, Revenant... Mr. Tin Man. All we can do is to accept these changes.
Hey, Doctor, you good?
Hmm, I should rein in the water and not let it enter the respiratory tract...
Or could it be... that my skills have gotten rusty after not using them for so long?
The Doctor probably bumped that hood on something and passed out.
Ahh—so sorry!
It's all cause of that Justin Jr., he suddenly dumped a stack of papers on me, said all the directors have to fill them out or we won't get our funding for next year.
That was close, I almost didn't make it.
Oh, you're awake, Doctor.
Good morning... no, good evening.

opts: I remember being in a ventilation duct—

lines.x: Yup. I was manipulating my water around when I knew you were in trouble.
The two of you were about to be discovered when I found you, so I wrapped you up in water and brought you here.
I was in a hurry, so excuse the bumpy ride, 'kay?

opts: I can't repay the kindness you've shown me.

lines.x: You've saved me once, and now I got you back... Oh, who knows whose turn it'll be next?
Doctor, you're not thinking of how you're gonna repay me, are you?
Um... how about you go shopping with me next time?
It's not that great a deal, hehe.
But you know, if you insist on returning the favor, just sign this employment contract I got here! I wouldn't mind having an assistant with brains as big as yours.
Doctor... I know you just woke up, but, I gotta tell you some bad news.
It seems like Saria got attacked at the same time you were.
I'm not sure about the specifics, but that's Saria, you know? Harder than diamonds! She wouldn't be in any serious danger, right?
The big question is what you do next. You must've gotten the feeling that once the military gives you a warning, they don't give up so easily.
So your situation here is... Actually, Rhodes Island's situation is far from hunky-dory.
Iffy should be fine and dandy, but Saria...
I'm not sure about the specifics, but that's Saria, you know? Harder than diamonds! She wouldn't be in any serious danger, right?
The big question is what you do next. You must've gotten the feeling that once the military gives you a warning, they don't give up so easily.
So your situation here is... Actually, Rhodes Island's situation is far from hunky-dory.
Outside...
The corridor... someone is walking toward us.
Huh?
But I was so cautious on the way here, the army couldn't possibly have tailed us that easily.
Or could it be the Maylander guys? Like... Mr. Tin Can and his pets...? Secret agents like to pop up like this, don't they?

opts: Rosmontis, is it the Tin Man?

lines.x: I don't know.
I don't feel anything special... the feeling I'm getting is... um, very normal...

opts: Muelsyse, can you take a look around the area?

lines.x: Hm? You want me to send a few 'eyes' out there? You got it.
Huff...*pant*...finally...
Sorry, am I late?
I wanted to see you as soon as you got to Trimounts. Unfortunately... my work took much longer than expected.
You're...
Wait, I remember that mug from the docs... You're Loken Williams?!
Lo...ken?
Yes. It is I.
You must have received my letter, Narcissa.
Who is Narcissa?
My name is Rosmontis. It's my only name.
You... no matter. What's important is that you've come. To tell the truth, I've been a bit nervous ever since I sent the letter.
To see that you're willing to meet me... makes me a very happy man.
You wrote the letter? You wanted me to come here?
Yes, my child. I even wanted to go looking for you myself.
But time is short, so I had no choice but to have a friend deliver that letter to... Rhodes Island. Yes, Rhodes Island, that's what it was.
And after the disaster... I heard that organization took you in. Have they been treating you well?
They are... my family.
Dr. Kal'tsit, Amiya, the Doctor, Blaze, Raidian... and everyone in the squad, they're all my family.
So you mean... you've found a new family...*cough* *cough*
The frail man coughs violently, and his naturally crooked body seems to droop even lower.
He stares at Rosmontis, as if wanting to go move to her, but a thin wall of water stops him in his tracks.
Muelsyse knows who he is, and so do you. This is the very same name that cropped up many times in Rosmontis's files.
The criminal that should have been well behind bars has suddenly appeared before you, acting as a grandparent rushing to visit his grandchild after work.

opts: You came here alone... to find Rosmontis?

lines.x: This is what I wanted. Nothing to do with anyone else.
Let me... let me get a good look at you...
You've grown up, girl.
Over the past four years... no, it should be five soon enough...? I've thought of you so many times while we've been apart.
Whenever I think about the time we were separated from each other, I just feel so... sorry.
*cough* *cough*...... *cough* *cough* *cough*...
I'm sorry... that I couldn't protect you from them. Those mediocre hacks with their superficial goals... they destroyed the future we could have had together.
A future? Were we supposed to live together?
But I don't remember you.
Um... should I have remembered this? I record everything I don't want to forget in my terminal.
You've forgotten who I am? Have you lost all your memories, and forgotten the wrongs I've done?
Are you a bad man? Did you do something bad to me?
That's not for me to answer, my child. It would make this meeting meaningless.
Your memory loss... it should not have been an effect of the experiments. Could there have been another party? Maylander, or perhaps Rhine?
Don't aim those eyes my way, Mr. Williams. I'm just the teensy weensy Director of Ecological.
Besides, even if the old goat was here, his precarious code of ethics wouldn't be any worse than yours, right?
This has nothing to do with ethics.
Rhodes Island, did you wipe the girl's memories and manipulate her emotions?

opts: I won't dignify that with a response.
It's not something Rhodes Island would do.

lines.x: Yes, you couldn't have done that. It was my work after all... I was so meticulous that no one could have possibly stripped away such an important part of her when her vitals were functioning normally.

Oh, I see now... it was me after all? Perhaps I didn't make enough observations... Yes... We didn't have the luxury of time... The pressure of consciousness is honestly beyond anything a layman could comprehend...
Or could it be... that you passed your memories onto your sibling?
Um...
I... don't know how to answer you.
There must be a way, let me think...
Any fragment of that incident is beyond meaningless, same as the records and data, they all belong in the garbage dump. But emotions, shapes, scents... oh yes, smells, that's it.
A... floral scent?
This smells like... crushed juice... smeared on the walls, and on my hands...
No... no...
Stop, stop thinking about it! I don't want this!
Yes, I knew it! I'm still somewhere in your memories!
Please, child, bring out those memories, remember your hatred for me, let your anger fill your body, your mind, your omnipresent spirit!
Ugh...
Hurting her?
No, her pain comes from the past, not the present. I only want to give her the gift she's been longing for.
Do you actually understand her pain?
Everything you know about her and her experiences is second-hand information from Maylander. Don't you think she has the right to be angry, to hold resentment?
Oh, I know. Haha, both you and Maylander are walking the same path. You want her easy to control, because you're afraid of her, afraid of...
...A weapon of reignited anger.
'Weapon.'
Everything you experienced with Rosmontis in Chernobog comes flooding back before your eyes. The fights desperate with determination to live alongside the pain of farewells.
Yes, you've heard the term used many a time in the past to describe the Feline girl by your side. She felt it was harsh before, but this time it's unbearable.
Because it came out of Loken Williams's mouth, the root cause that led Rosmontis here in the first place.
You have many ideas on how to refute his claims. It also occurs to you that if Kal'tsit were here, she would be capable of coming up with an even more extensive response.
But another thought comes to mind before your words.
Gah...*cough*...haha...
I see you're outraged by my words, that's fine. You're not willing to think of her as a 'weapon.' But, just look at her... She's standing in front of you, even wielding a sword for you.
Gah...*cough*...haha...
I see you're outraged by my words, that's fine. You're not willing to think of her as a 'weapon.' But, just look at her... She's standing in front of you, even wielding a sword for you.
Doctor, please get behind me.
My sword might be about to pierce through the building's outer wall.
Enemies.
There are enemies closing in quickly.
I will protect you, Doctor. My mind still feels like... it's a mess. up. But Dr. Kal'tsit and Amiya told me my mental state is a lot more stable now. They allowed me to protect you.
And this is one thing I can definitely do.
Doctor... of Rhodes Island.
If you truly care for her, then you should know what she really needs.
Just ask yourself this... what are you trying to protect? A soldier that would die for you, or a child with a traumatic past?
The question is not difficult to answer, but it is obvious that Loken is not interested in your reply.
From the moment he stepped into the room, he has been staring directly at the white Feline girl next to you, his turbid gaze full of enthusiasm, sorrow, and a certain kind of anticipation.
They're here!
Such a pity that this is the end of our meeting.
You're not ready yet, my child... but I know you will be.
I'll be waiting for you at 'home.'
Hold it.
You actually caught up... no, you were waiting for me.
Which one of you is your true self? The silent one in the room earlier, or the one right here?
That's not important.
Your Originium Arts are quite interesting, or perhaps... no, forget it, I don't have time for this curiosity.
Do you have business with me, Director of Rhine Lab?
Where's Kristen?
Ah...... but you're the Rhine one here. Are you sure you should be asking me that?
The only way you could have gotten out of prison is with her help.
Your being here means the work she entrusted to you is already complete.
She's done preparing, right? She's really planning to...
Pitiful girl.
Hey...
I'm not about to let a rotten geezer who looks like he hasn't showered in three days call me pitiful.
Astonished, unwilling, and anxious... you're afraid, little girl. You're afraid of being betrayed by those you trust, and of being abandoned by others.
Well, the worst is your confusion... You're at such a complete loss that you have no choice but to ask an 'outsider' like me.
Such a familiar feeling it is...*cough*, *cough* *cough*...
When my teacher handed all my research to Maylander, when he expelled me from the academy...
When my students submitted all my data to the military before I did, and I had to speed up my experiments, leading to that ultimate failure...
When my family sold everything we had in D.C. and disappeared...
I saw that very same look in the mirror.
...My eye bags can't be that bad?
I just don't get it... why would she trust that wretched woman from Maylander and you over Saria, or... ugh....
Are you stalling for time here? If you're waiting for Ho'olheyak to pick you up, I won't necessarily lose, you know?
You don't want me for an enemy, girl.
...Huh?
Do you really think you could stop Kristen and I just by catching up to me?
Or, maybe...
You want to catch me, to catch... Kristen?
Muelsyse falls into a daze. Loken turns his back and yet she still doesn't act.
Only when the familiar wind caresses her face so mockingly does she realize that Loken is no longer anywhere to be seen on the streets.
I let him go... what am I doing...?
Oh, Doctor.
I'm... I'm right here, heard everything you said crystal clear, yessiree.

opts: Did your clone manage to stop Loken?

lines.x: Clone...
My... *ahem* Yes, my clone outside.
Doctor, you guessed right. They couldn't have let such a terminally ill old man come here by himself.
Before his assistants got him away, I only managed to get one thing—Loken Williams is definitely involved in Kristen's plans.

opts: The secret that Maylander and the army are scrambling for.
And they're pointing right at Kristen Wright's hideout.

lines.x: Doctor, your terminal... there's a new message?

opts: Maylander has some new information to share with us.

lines.x: What a coinkydink, I was just wondering when we were gonna see the Tin Man again.
You got roughed up by the army because of him, so it's only right that he should make it up to you, isn't it?

opts: Rosmontis, are you ready to proceed with the mission?

lines.x: Yeah, I'm fine.
...Huh?
Ah... haha... our cooperation is far from over, so you're going to wear out those words if keep standing on ceremony, you know?
Doctor... do you really trust me so much?
Nothing. Alright, let's get moving.
Silence, is the temperature okay... not too hot?
You're controlling it very well.
Saria's body temperature has stabilized, and she should be out of danger.
Ifrit, you should rest for a bit. Using Originium Arts for a long time will wear you out.
No, I'm staying with Saria.
I still remember back in the white room... she kept me company when I was stuck in that bed.
I was always in pain, my mind was totally messed up, and I couldn't stop myself from burning everything I saw.
I don't even have a clear picture of every visit, I just know that every time I opened my eyes, she would there at my bedside, and I felt so relieved.
So now I want to stay here and keep her company. Even though she won't open her eyes... I'm sure she can feel that I'm here.
...I'm sure she can.
She just needs... a bit more sleep.
Um...
Something still feels off.
What's wrong?
I just realized Saria isn't that much taller than I am. And also... her palms are soft, with such pretty nails too. Just like your hands, Silence.
Yes.
Saria is... of course, an ordinary human.
I'm afraid she's quite far from 'ordinary.'
Director Jara...
She prefers to solve problems by herself, without a word. She always keeps a straight face that deters people from voicing their opinions.
Would you like to make a guess just how many employees from Defense I had to offer psychological counseling year after year?
Are you... unhappy with Saria?
Unhappy? Hmph.
One year I asked Kristen if she was set on transferring a researcher to make the head of the Defense Section—Guess what the woman there did when she found out?
She threw three dozen listening devices onto my desk, and right in my face, shattered them to pieces with her newly developed Arts.
An unfortunate thing, as a couple of them were operating on behalf of some of our dear government departments.
They were just a little wary of such a rapidly-progressing tech company.
I was happy to share some 'controllable' information with them, to get them to ease up a little.
I spent some time dealing with the trouble that came with the incident... but who could deny the determination and skill of that young Vouivre?
I guess... Saria has always been like that.
Sure enough, many years have come and gone, and if you compare her then and now, it doesn't seem like there's been much development.
She's the most resolute and determined woman I know.
Who ever said she wasn't? I've watched she and Kristen walk their paths, and I'm well aware just how decisive and strong she is.
But even such an outstanding individual can't stand against... the whole damned country. If you let her take on these obstacles she hasn't overcome in all this time, she'll only be smashed to bits.
Here.
Car keys...?
Take Saria and Ifrit and leave, before it's too late.
Saria... wouldn't want that.
No, she wouldn't. But I'm not talking to Saria right now.
Aren't you the type to go 'innocent lives shouldn't be put at risk?' This girl shouldn't be involved, and neither should you. No one needs to die here.
I need... a bit more time.
Director Jara?
I'm afraid we're all out of time. Take the secret passageway out, now.
What's wrong?
An old friend has come knocking.
This conference room...
Whenever we had meetings, Parvis would sit by the window, Muelsyse likes to sit in the middle, and you... Director Jara, you sit here, furthest from Kristen.
And you're the same as before, always standing behind somebody else's seat.
Hm... how nostalgic.
Would you like something to drink? Red wine, or should we pop open the champagne?
Is there an occasion?
Of course. Mr. Ferdinand Clooney is enjoying a career upgrade, and also returned to Rhine Lab... Sounds like a cause for celebration?
You think I'm here as the army's guide?
No, they have their own plans for Rhine, and I don't approve at all. Because I know that if something happens to the Vice President while he's here, that's the end of the company.
Kristen can ignore the existence of Rhine Lab, but not me.
The event today went off without a hitch.
Don't play dumb with me, Jara.
You may not know just what kind of people run the army. They could repeat today's adventure anytime they want.
So before those savages rip a dozen years' worth of time and effort to shreds, can you just tell me the truth—what exactly is Kristen doing?
Did Colonel Blake not tell you?
The very first day Dr. Wright disappeared, I handed every document about Control and her projects right over to them.
And for the record, it happened more than once. I worked days of overtime just for this.
Heh, 'Dr. Wright,' as if you two have the most ordinary collaborative relationship.
Should I show Blake the picture hidden in the darkest corner of your office drawer?
Or has our dear HR Director already come up with an explanation for why she's hidden the New Year's card little Kristen wrote her two decades ago?
...That's just an old photo.
Kids aren't as cute and well-behaved after they grow up. Ferdinand, you know that better than me. That sock in the jaw at Site 359 still smarting?
...Heh.
She's gone into hiding, like a child clinging to her favorite toy. Except it's not a toy, it's a goddamned superweapon, with some real forbidden secrets behind it.
So you should find and drag her out, if you still care about her.
Or would you rather watch her die for the vaguest of ideals?
Even if... I couldn't bear to watch her go that far ahead, how do you suppose that stopping her is within my power?
This is Kristen we're talking about, Ferdinand. She'll take everything you have.
Do you think she would ever leave such a major weakness—if you really want to treat the relationship between me and her as a vulnerability—so easily within your grasp?
Good... great. You refuse to cooperate.
And what about the others? For example...
Saria.
You rescued her, and you've hidden her somewhere around here.
You're so willing to talk to me, just to buy her more time to escape.
Let's say she manages to escape. Who could she even go to?
Rhodes Island, caught up in the same mess, or that group of Maylander agents who love spinning webs just as much as you do?
...I have no idea what you're talking about.
The military just picked up a new piece of intelligence.
Saria... as well as the company behind her, Rhodes Island, are suspected of helping Kristen steal classified information pertaining to national security.
Maybe working at Rhine Lab for more than a decade has made you naive enough to... assume your old boss was some hero of justice?
Hmm... the usual stench of the sewers, and spots where the light doesn't reach.
These alleys are all the same.
Urban planners probably can't afford to invent new ways to do their jobs at the speed these cities pop up out of the barrenlands.
It's just too bad that you miss out on the local scenery if you use these nooks and crannies too much.
Am I right, my good subordinate? Isn't it better to get a view of the city from above?
A gust of wind blows through the empty alley.
All the un-exhaled smoke rings swirl back into the cracks in his metal. The Tin Man wonders if this is an opportune time to hack a few coughs as if choking.
A lighter floats right in front of him as soon as the embers of his cigarette die out.
'Banal' is such a lamentable assessment for a city like this with no history.
...Ho'olheyak.
You were supposed to make contact three days ago.
Oh? I had the impression that you're not particularly punctual yourself.
If you could just tone down your volume. Why did you pick this place for our meeting?
Did you forget? The factory in District 13 is close by.
Did you find the item from the other day?
Yes, but unfortunately there are only fragments left. Fragments of... stars, with no way to guide the lost.
And Kristen? Where is she?
Well...
Are you watching the sky?
You get a clearer view of the sky without the skyscrapers around.
Do you think Kristen might be hiding in a star up there? If so, then how are we supposed to find her?
Ho'olheyak, you went into the library two months ago.
I'm a historian, isn't it basically my job to visit libraries?
We're talking about Maylander's 'library' here.
I don't recall authorizing that.
You know I can't contain my curiosity.
Just like right now...
If I could trouble my employer with... a question that I've been awfully curious about...
What is it?
Your smoke.
Other than the clouds you puff out, the few wisps that slip into your body... where exactly do they go?
Is this why... you have the tip of your tail on my chest?
I smell my favorite scent on you... history.
So if it's at all possible, I'd love to get a good look at every little piece of your body for my research... Where should I start? Your head? Chest? Extremities?
...Ho'olheyak.
Do you really think... betraying the Maylander Foundation is the right choice here?
Of course... not.
Maylander holds most of this country's secrets. And in Columbia, secrets can be traded for wealth... or used as murder weapons.
I spent three whole years before I finally managed to get into the Historical Society, and spent another three to gain your trust.
Do you know what six years means to me? Being a Maylander agent has already taken... one-seventh of my life.
Then you shouldn't be wasting the rest of it behind bars or on the run.
Yes, yes... I know.
And yet... if my time here isn't enough to embrace what I've been yearning for my whole life, then what difference does it make if I live another ten years or one more minute?
Maylander won't allow anyone, least of all a defected agent, to access information they shouldn't be accessing.
