Is this the entirety of Loken's research?
No, there's another address... a blurred one. It looks familiar. You've really done a lot right under Maylander's nose. I'm starting to actually respect you.
What a shame.
What's that?
You won't see the sky that you should have gotten to see.
The stars? My people have played with them long enough. I've given you all the information I should. Do you really think those twisted spots of light are worth looking at?
No, not the sky we see today.
Its true aspect, in the future.
Enough. I know about your great plans, but that future doesn't belong to me. As long as your data is legit, I'll be able to fulfill my ancestors' dream and recover my true power—
The power that you desire could not even overcome time. You understand that it could be machines that fly the skies of the future, or other evolving lifeforms...
But not a shadow from ten thousand years ago.
Are you mocking me... mocking my blood, my memory, my destiny?
You—
Sacrificing your entire race just to return to the past... is it really worth it?
Whatever. I've got what I wanted. Let's say goodbye in peace.
Ho'olheyak jumps down the monitoring platform.
She does not turn around to look at Kristen. She does not want Kristen to see the look on her face.
You.
We meet again.
They are silent for a moment.
Past and future pass by each other, without making contact.

lines.st: Five minutes.

lines.x: I can't get the door open!
Here.
Huh?
It's... the weapon you lent me?
Not lent. Gave. It's yours. You just need practice.
Practice... er...
You're free, once you go out from here. The agents and soldiers are too busy to bother. They've found H.A.M.H.R.R. and achieved their goal. They won't bother you anymore.
Run to Trimounts. There, you'll still be Researcher Silence.
Um...
You want to go in the most dangerous direction, don't you? You could put your life in danger. You may never be able escape Maylander's gaze ever again.
It'll be a struggle of a lifetime. You may lose some things forever. Freedom, the life you know, the trust of your family and friends, the right to live under the sun, and yourself.
Are you sure you want a life like that?
I don't know. I haven't seen the problem resolved with my own eyes. I don't want to give up halfway.
You're a talented scholar, a good doctor. Have you made up your mind to become a warrior?
Maybe I'll never be a warrior.
But I'll grasp the weapon you gave me tight, if necessary.
Very well, child. Go forth with my blessing.
Thank you, Director Jara.
By the way, Control... Kristen could be just ahead.
Don't you want to see her?
Do I? Of course I do.
But I doubt I can catch her.
Jara gazes at the laboratory.
Then, she turns her gaze toward the sky.
You got it yet?
Not yet!
Keep going. The core of H.A.M.H.R.R. should be in here.
And Kristen... she couldn't have escaped right under our noses.

lines.st: Three minutes.

lines.x: ...Ifrit?
Saria! Did you find a way in?
Not yet.
This place is weird. Even after Rosmontis crushed most of the outer wall like a cookie, it's not revealing any of the stuff inside.
...It's not a complete building.
What?
You're right, Ifrit. It's not a lab.
This is... it's...
Did she install her parents' propulsion device on... how could it be? No... how did I not think of it until now?
Saria! Where are you going?
If I break the wall...
She must have expected me. It's pointless.
Then what has a point?
You can't change anything.
Would we be too late?
No.
I have to...
Have to what?
Saria.
Si...lence?
You need to get out of here... it could be...
No.
Saria, I didn't come here to listen to you talk like that.
I know how far a scientist would go for her dream. I know that feeling of rapture, which is why I know the strength it takes to fight it with morality.
You've long been seeking a balance between science and order. You do well; perhaps too well.
Your order is falling apart. But if you're willing to let balance go... perhaps you'll find your roots amidst the chaos.
Find my... roots?
I believe you can.
We have all things to do. Let's part here.
I can tell you now, Saria.

lines.st: How much I looked up to you.
How much I wanted to walk by your side.
One minute.

lines.x: Well, there goes a rare friend.
How do you plan on making it up to me, Kristen?

lines.st: Thirty seconds.

lines.x: Narcissa... You've returned. Are you ready to pronounce judgment?
Yes, I want to kill you.
Not for revenge. I hate you, but Amiya and the Doctor both say that revenge won't bring back the ones I love.
Not for destruction. You want to use me to destroy you. I won't. I'm not your weapon.
You're just a criminal. You're nothing to me.
I'll do what the law and prison could not do.
I hereby sentence you to death.
Good... good.
How does that song go again?
Country roads♪ Take me home♪
To the place I belong♪
Soon. Very soon.
Severe tremors shake the earth.
Like something very strong and resilient is about to break out.
You gaze at the rising inner corridor, and see a figure through the windows.
Kristen.
A name that has been on many lips these days.
Lunatic. Genius. Foe. Friend.
Or perhaps a dreamer who does not belong to this time.
You see her with your own eyes for the first time. She continues to look up at the sky, as though the blank emptiness is the only thing she should care about.

opts: You cannot remain on your feet!

lines.x: A familiar silhouette appears.
Mon3tr!
(Screeches)
It unfolds in an instant, grabbing your shoulders lightly with sharp claws, the massive figure covering you fully.
Then, an even more massive shadow rises behind Mon3tr.
H.A.M.H.R.R..
Now you can see that the mysterious ring corridor and Muelsyse's vivarium are both part of H.A.M.H.R.R..
Maylander and the military fought over it, then made peace over it, but no one could have predicted that it would reveal itself in this way.
The bright flames of the thrusters feel blinding. The blast throws you off your feet.
If not for Kal'tsit and Mon3tr, you would have been cast to the winds, like those around you, and the broken remains of the lab.
Hang on, Doctor.

opts: I didn't know Kristen's lab could fly.
We were so close to the truth...

lines.x: I know.
The figure in the air casts her gaze at you.
No, not at you. The dark monster that is protecting you.
(Growls)
Kristen moves her lips, seemingly inquiringly.
You hear Kal'tsit repeat her answer to you.
—I know.
November 21st, 1099
7:11 P.M., H.A.M.H.R.R. Mission Elapsed Time 00:03:00
Brr... ugh...
Phooie, freezing around here. Asshats keep shutting off the heaters.
How many complaints am I gonna have to file before they realize we're here at night on overtime?
Hey, honey, what are you calling for?
Hold on... I'll speak to you outside.
What day is it today?
Um... nine days out from the project deadline. Progress is looking bad for us.
I won't be home today either, no.
Our anniversary...? Oof.
Sorry, it's been crazy the past couple days... I know, honey, you've been looking forward to that restaurant forever... I know, I know, I'm sorry.
The VP was touring Rhine Lab, so the egghead idiots on the Board have been going insane.
They're forcing every project to produce interim results immediately, or else we're SOL.
I'm not coming up with excuses, honey, it's just... Listen, I can't do today, I'll make it up to you some other time, I swear.
Hello? Honey, are you there?
...Shit.
If only something would just... happen.
Why won't the offices blow up? Why won't goons rob a bank? Why don't Trimounts's stupid city treads just snap in half...
Anything.
Night gradually sinks in. The office lights are set for another all-nighter, the shadow caging in this one pitiable researcher.
He gives a shiver. They say snow's coming in a few weeks. Someone bumps past him out of nowhere.
Okay, my fault for hoping.
Eventually, he shuffles his feet, stepping back into the high-rise. Just as he's stifled his urge to stay outside a little longer, an irritating droning noise emits from far off.
Catastrophe alarms, sounding with no warning. A PA demanding evacuation echoes through the streets. The researcher subconsciously leans to hurry his gait.
But suddenly, he notices everyone around him coming to a slow halt, all gazes drawn at once towards the sky.
With great skepticism, he lifts his head again.
—What the... hell is that?
A massive toroid, in sluggish revolution, rises up from behind the building.
In the dark, as mechanical devices unfold, the neon of the city that glares off the upper surface becomes one with the structure, metal luster shining too brightly for the naked eye.
It is so imposingly majestic, so impossibly elegant, that it could only be the stuff of the most beautiful dreams ever dreamt. It pierces through the clouds. And inexplicably... it rises still.
Some kind of... ring?
7:13 P.M., H.A.M.H.R.R. mid-flight
I'm very interested, of course, extremely interested.
Really? Well, that's swell! If our project can get Rhine sponsorship...
Take a seat, ma'am. Anything to drink?
Uh... plain water will be fine, Justin, sir.
Please, call me Justin Jr., even.
How about some wine? War took the Gaulish Empire, but I thank the troops for leaving us the best vineyards intact.
Please.
I-If you insist. Thank you.
Come in.
Director, your office phone has been ringing non-stop.
Tell them I'm busy.
You have... some friends of yours looking for you.
Mayor Benjamin, Councilman Chris, Deputy Phil, Commander Colette. Who else?
You have a Mr. Lewis. The one in charge of industrial sec.
Hmph.
They say—Who might you be, ma'am?
Ah, sorry! I'll wait outside!
Don't worry about it, no secrets here.
Go ahead.
Er, right.
They appear to be extremely unhappy with the articles you deposited at Plate Seven. They were under the impression the project was a more—'ordinary' one.
Commander Colette went to Mr. Lewis. I'm afraid all Plate Seven monitoring files will be lost by tomorrow.
The Mayor and Councilman have returned every case we've sent them over the past few years, and declared they'll no longer be seeking your aid for campaigns.
The Deputy made some very... radical statements. I hear he's gotten an emergency arrest warrant for the state prosecutor, and seems to be on the way here now.
I've notified Defense, but we can't keep them away for long.
And here I swore we got along well enough.
*sigh* How fragile a friendship is. Wouldn't you say, ma'am?
Justin, sir, I-I'm only here to ask you to invest, I don't think I should necessarily be in the loop on all this...

Aren't you curious, though? What could Rhine Lab have that scares these elites, and bureaucrats, and black suits in white gloves, so badly that they'd crawl out of bed in the middle of the night to get to work?
I... I don't know. A-A nanodroid that extends your lifespan forever... or something?
Nice idea. If someone came to me with a prospectus and technical prototype, you know I'd invest.
Ha... haha...
Um, I am wondering a little—
—why you'd call me up, of all people...
Have you ever heard of Stade syndrome, ma'am?
Huh? I-I've seen it in the files. A rare hereditary condition, I think?
Very rare, highly fatal, and extremely stubborn. Sufferers of it aren't usually expected to live beyond forty-five, and more than likely pass it on to their children.
I assume it was Rhine Lab that developed—
Look, no rush to win your potential sponsor over.
But, yes, fairytale ending, there's a miracle cure out for Stade syndrome, and while Rhine Lab had no involvement in the research, it goes without saying that many have reaped the benefits.
Pity it arrived a little late, is all. Old Justin Sr. died of organ failure from Stade syndrome three days before they finalized the drug.
Justin Sr.? He must be...
Sure was. And his son, Justin Jr., was informed out of the big wide blue, after struggling in fear for a decade and change, that he could live like a normal human thanks to the med.
Bam. The nightmare that plagued my family was gone. Like it never even existed.
Only later did I learn that the key element in the drug popped up when an intern researcher happened to mess up some reagents.
That's fate. Hah, that's how it goes.
I... um, well, at least you're free of the disease now.
Because of a random, dumb mistake?
The incapable might call it 'a twist of fate' and when the curtains are closed, pat themselves on the back for it, even.
They see it as a gift of chance.
But I—
I can't stand it. I won't stand for it.
I'll have this phenomenon safe and secure in my own hands. I'll grip it. I'll control it.
I'll dictate to it where it goes.
A Catastrophe alarm? Why does it have to be now...?
Justin, sir, we need to evacuate!
Ha, finally.
Justin Fitzroy Jr.
Well, if it isn't the Supreme Court, you've finally got me.
What is your pledge?
I pledge it will lead Columbia forth.
What guarantees that?
Is my participation not enough?
Your many 'friends' are dissuading me at this very moment.
They tell me Columbian law shouldn't allow your people continue in their unbridled ways.
If a mere fondness for sending out cases of money does unbridled ways make, then what, pray tell, do Kristen's actions sum to?
Justice, you told me that it's not for the protection of any sanctimonious 'ethics' that Columbian law works.
It must hew the brambles, for those who make forth.
I ask, then. What is your verdict?
If Columbia's court pales timid and weak, then I should see no reason why it deserves my further tenure.
Much obliged.
Was that just a... a Supre—Supreme Court Justice calling?
What... research are you... are Rhine Lab even handling?
Easy, easy. Rhine Lab's no danger to the law. You're with a trustworthy firm.
But...
Ah, well, back on topic. About what I've been stashing in the jurisdictional care of my 'friends.'
Go pull the curtains, ma'am. You won't want to miss the show tonight.
What is... that?
How is something that big... flying?
Is that... another Rhine Lab project?

I'll be honest, you're not really a qualified entrepreneur. You're a flip-flopper. Scare easy. Not confident enough, always second-guessing. Got a habit of spending too much time on how you word things.
To be frank, it's no wonder Trimountian investor circles don't like you.
Lucky for you, none of that matters to me.
Rhine Lab will buy out your power laboratory in whole, I'll provide you ample funds, and it will be seen to that all is done to support your research endeavors to come.
If Lady Luck is on our side, and one day you rise to directorship at Rhine Lab, then it's not out of the question that you might get a little section carved out for you.
Just as our young Dr. Franks got.
That's... I don't understand, so much has happened today...
Just say yes. Because luck doesn't claim the credit here, for it has been my decision.
Where the hand of Justin Jr. reaches, there is random chance no longer, ma'am.
I help the lunatic to succeed.
...I understand, Director Justin.
Looking forward to working with you.
And welcome to the Rhine Lab family. Cheers.
7:25 P.M., H.A.M.H.R.R. mid-flight
Seems we've finally found it, Colonel.
Bitch of a process, eh?
We weren't anticipating Kristen would have another propulsion unit ready to go for H.A.M.H.R.R.
She hid the whole contraption under a garbage heap, and now she's sailed the damn thing through Trimounts airspace like she owns it.
This is all my fault.
No point in talking the blame now, Colonel.
Things are all headed for the worst-case.
H.A.M.H.R.R. is still flying for S.H.A.F.T., bound for its upper surface with an ETA of twenty minutes.
Based on what the manuals say, it'll be hovering within about five klicks of the S.H.A.F.T., which gives it maximal odds of taking down any ground targets in its angle of drift.
But of course, 'Arc-01' is in no suitable position to launch attacks or counter enemy positions right now. Our plans never extended beyond demonstrated feasibility within Trimounts.
Five klicks of altitude is something the average aerial unit just can't do. Your considerations were all-comprehensive outside of that blindsiding.
We're dispatching specialist drones from the District capable of higher altitudes, but we still need more time.
Meanwhile, we have every regular drone allocated to Trimounts already on liftoff. They'll interfere with H.A.M.H.R.R.'s continued ascent as best they can.
Effects look to be limited.
Seems the military gave this ring thing some sturdy armor. I thought these Maylander drones were advanced for Columbia as is.
Outstanding work. Medals for all the generals, I say.
The DOD has a hell of a lot resting on this.
...'No point in talking the blame now,' sir.
What's important is we get the situation back into your hands.
Into 'our' hands, Colonel. Personally, I understand the pitch the army is making. Ever since the Bolívar debacle, your people have been mighty eager to get back on the stage.
But we're all Columbians, aren't we?
Goes with saying, when I enlisted, I pledged my loyalty to Columbia.
Kristen still won't contact us, and won't respond to any hails from us either.
These aren't the actions of an opportunist or kidnapper, but if this is a bluff by the military, we can't say anything for sure.
All we can be thankful for is—no matter what she intends to do, the S.H.A.F.T. is under our control and always has been. She has no way to...
Sorry, Mr. Vice President, let me proceed.
Colonel, it seems your people have some urgent news for you.
It's me. I'm with the Vice President right...
...Charging?
Haven't we been in total control of the S.H.A.F.T. this whole time? How could it... Shut the whole S.H.A.F.T. down immediately!
You can't? What do you mean you can't? How hard can it be to find the goddamned switch or breaker or whatever it is and flip it?
Pull out the wires! Kill the power source! Do whatever it takes, I don't care what facilities we damage!
...What?
You're telling me, you've done everything you can, and the S.H.A.F.T. has not only failed to shut down, its goddamned energy readings are still climbing?
Bring every ounce of explosive we have in Trimounts. Blow that thing to kingdom come.
It's already charged, and if you blow it up, the energy dissipation will level the entire city, Colonel. That's what Maylander's agents are telling me.
You've got a crafty one on your hands, hah.
She's out of her goddamned mind! How much does she hate Columbia?! What is she up to?!
Hold on... I can think of one possibility.
—The District.
It's the District. If H.A.M.H.R.R.'s angle deviates far enough, it's probably gonna threaten Max D.C.!
Our beloved President is still situated there. Kristen... she intends to destroy the heart and the brain of Columbia.
We have to evacuate all personnel in the District immediately.
Don't worry, Colonel. The President's just told me we're fine.
Armed forces in the District are mobilized, and General Barry is taking charge of the defense there. She's promised me she'll protect the President with her life.
...General Barry?
Oh, I guess you didn't hear. She's just been promoted.
Carry on with your mission, Colonel. Columbia still needs you in this dire hour.
Anything else?
No, nothing.
I swear, I'm gonna put an end to this insanity, personally.
Don't overwork yourself.
...If you'll excuse me.
Will these orders do, Mr. President?
I'll be honest, I'm not entirely sure about this. Still, I won't question your judgment—
I understand. I'll head out at once.
You see that? What is it?
Is that what the brass is after?
Sure is a high-flying bastard...
Where's it heading?
...Out of the city.
By appearances, an energy accumulation device.
Out of the city? The S.H.A.F.T.?
What did the Colonel call those things? 'Arc-01?' H.A.M.H.R.R.?
What the hell is that mad scientist thinking?
What the hell is that mad scientist thinking?
Idealism. Obsession. Curiosity. Initiative.
It's a trend that scientists are tied to these qualities.
Silence will admit, as a scientist herself, as a former Rhine Lab employee, that when she first caught that tremendous megastructure rising to the sky, she felt a faint tug on her heartstrings.
And a mortifying sense of pride.
This company she once struggled for was creating—no—had created a wonder of the land... of the sky. She should by all rights bask in it... shouldn't she?
Deception. Calculation. Sacrifice. Conspiracy.
She quickly comes to, realizing what this marvel was built upon.
Control. Kristen Wright...
As an ordinary researcher, she never had too much contact with Kristen. Perhaps, at most, a glimpse or two of the Rhine Lab Control during wrap-up conferences at the end of the year.
She still remembers the look in Kristen's eyes.
In her eyes, there was only one thing.
Scientists must think about one thing, and one thing only... 'And that is how to accomplish it.'
A scientist's sole responsibility is to push science to progress. 'That alone is more than enough to exhaust us.'
...And where is it that progress lies?

Whenever Columbia's wastes see rain, the vegetation quickly turns to overgrowth. When people hand down decisions from high perches, dictating what plants are of use and what should be disparaged as weeds, it never sits well with me.
We need to admit, no matter what, that that should not be described as 'progress.'
Kristen, and those like her, and those who wear the same guise as her...
They all lay claim to their vision, their direction, as the way that science's progress should orient.
She wouldn't dare say she understands the secretive, elusive Control, but she understands the rest of them, and the respective... costs of their journeys forward.
Who will define progress? Who will watch over it... Hasn't it seen too many sacrifices beneath its heels?
Science must progress, of course.
I am a researcher myself. I sympathize as such.
But we must be aware—of the nature of the costs therein.
We must be aware—
—that science does not solely serve personal ideals nor obsessions.
Is that a drone?
Researcher. What are you doing?
I don't want trouble with you, and the guys in charge don't need your help anymore, so you ought to—
Hey! We're talking to you!
The hell is she up to?
The key! Your car key!
She stole one of our cars? Is she insane?
Shit, we need to—
Silence! Silence!
Where'd the girl come from? This is a military exclusion zone!
The hell are you doing?!
Move it!
Ugh!
Gimme your car key too!
Uh, do—do you know how to drive, young lady?
Maybe you need me to—
Shut up! I've got this under control!
...Uh, but just to confirm, left one is the brake, right?
Doctor, we need to leave immediately.
I've found Kristen's energy source.
Things aren't yet irredeemable. The worst-case possibility I anticipate has not yet come to pass...
We still have a chance to stop her.
Kristen's plan requires mass amounts of energy, the scale and purity of which are both beyond any modern Terran's grasp, or even their imagination.

opts: So, she's exploiting the Sarcophagus's tech?

lines.x: The sheer vastness of this energy is more than a Sarcophagus can provide, too.
I said, more than 'a' Sarcophagus.
I hadn't anticipated... their being in Columbia.
Buried right beneath these wastes.
Doctor, I need your help.
And your help... is all I have.
Combat has concluded. She won't be in danger.
I ask you have faith in her. She may still be a child, but she is nevertheless a Rhodes Island Elite Operator.
And I ask you have faith... in the time you've spent with her.
Whether the operation in Chernobog, or her days aboard Rhodes Island, not one past moment has been for naught, and these experiences likewise mold her even now.
She will make her own choice, and we can only respect that choice.
But... right now, as it falls to her, so it falls to us too.
7:45 P.M., H.A.M.H.R.R. now at upper S.H.A.F.T.
Incredible.
I've always wondered how you learned to fight so well, busy with all those research projects.
And I thought I made efficient use of my time. Do you never sleep?
What are you doing here?
Ferdinand.
You took the words right out of my mouth, Saria. After forcibly entering a Trimountian military spec ops bureau, what could you possibly be doing?
Let me guess. You want to find something to chase after that airborne gizmo.
Maybe hijack a heavy duty army drone and tie yourself onto it, for example?
Or is that thinking a little too simple, Director Saria? I never took you for the impulsive type.
Out of the way, Ferdinand. I have no time to argue with you right now.

Would you look at that, how sad. The only three... well, fine, Scientific Investigation's doesn't count. The only two Directors Control left out of her incredible master plan just happen to have gathered here.
What do you want to do? Stop the madwoman?
Or... save her?
I have no need to tell you anything.
I assure you, I don't mean to criticize your relationship with Kristen.
I was just waiting for you.
Listen, Saria. I'm not here to get in your way.
I said, out of the way.
And I said, 'listen.'
...That's the sound of an aircraft.
Ferdinand, where is that one from?
The 'Horizon Arc Project' isn't just that superweapon Kristen's floating through the air. It's a complete strategic program.
I tapped into some connections to borrow an aircraft they use to service 'Arc-01,' and blew up the rest while I was at it.
Let me be clear. Look across all of Columbia, and you'll find very few passenger aircraft safely able to fly near barrier altitude.
They won't need much time to scramble high-altitude craft from the District, but for you, and for me, that time is more than enough.
You're proposing a collaboration.
You believe—that you're seriously qualified for this?
There's the same old Saria I know.
As though you'd have the ability to quell every single problem, even if I wasn't there to help.
But I don't have a single shred of doubt about this.
In your own words, there is no reason we should clash.
H.A.M.H.R.R. has reached the S.H.A.F.T.'s aerial space, and the S.H.A.F.T. has begun accumulating energy.
If we let this situation unfold as is, then no matter what Kristen's up to, this will end with the Department of Defense exposed to the winds of scrutiny.
And I'm sure we all know how Rhine Lab, as an 'accessory' to it, will end up.
And whether it be as a clandestine military proxy, or a former Director of Rhine Lab, you'll be finished, Ferdinand.
Exactly.
But I'm not about to give in.
We can still make it in time.
We can bring H.A.M.H.R.R. back down again in a safe descent before the energy excitation, and then there will be room to pull everything back.
And is it your life you're looking to redeem, or Rhine Lab?
Does it matter?
You'll have to be franker than that, Ferdinand.
You've been ready with this aircraft. You've been anticipating that Kristen could get this far.
Here I'd thought the busywork at Defense had ground away all your researcher's insight.
Still, I've been ready for less time than you think.
Do you honestly believe this adventure of yours will rescue anything?
If you didn't think so, then why would you be here?
Do you know what feeling has struck me deeper than anything else lately?
I feel like nobody cares about Rhine Lab's reputation.
Everyone else is either out to see what research funds Rhine can get them, or their only interest is in what Kristen's doing.
You may have considered how Kristen leaves the picture, but have you considered what will become of the company afterwards?
Yes, I know, you've all been captured by her charm and charisma.
With a little soul-searching, I have to wonder if I wasn't too? Of course I was.
But Rhine Lab is ours, of the blood we shed. I can't accept her setting fire to everything that I gave the sweat of my brow for.
No matter what reason drives it.
So I can swallow being a tuskbeast of the military, because I want to pull Rhine back from the brink. Is that so wrong?
I never thought I'd see you telling me this sort of thing.
They're very Director of Defense-like words, aren't they?
Frankly, I should be getting a cut of your salary for it.
I am no longer the Director of Defense.
Fine. Once the dust settles on all this, I'll talk with Jara about getting a raise.
You'll need to be reemployed first.
Still, you were right about one thing.
Rescuing Rhine... is far from my only aim.
I need Rhine, and Rhine needs me. That's my assessment, borne of reason and emotion.
However—
To put it bluntly, I'm curious.
This is Kristen we're talking about. We know Kristen. The army wants to think she's out to blow something up, which is stupid.
After working with her for so long, we know what kind of woman she is, and of course, you would understand her better than I ever could.
Saria, tell me straight. What's going on in that brain of hers?
How far is she going to take this?
I can conjecture.
Conjecture. Very cautious terminology.
Go on, then. Are you conjecturing or not?
Those drones currently on the attack may be unable to pierce H.A.M.H.R.R.'s armor for the time being, and cannot reach its altitude of hover.
But shooting down this maintenance aircraft of yours would be simple enough for them.

My guess is, the military and Maylander are deploying even greater firepower right now. Specialist drones capable of even higher altitudes. Even landing craft to convey troops. Their involvement in the front line is only a matter of time.
We need to get your little craft, with near to no armor and not a single weapon installed, straight through a net of drone fire.
This definitely carries a little risk.
I'll pilot.
Suit yourself.
Are you actually able to run this thing, though?
...I learned, once. In college.
7:50 P.M., H.A.M.H.R.R. altitude 3000 meters
Ungh...
It seems like... the Transmitter response... has started growing stronger.
It should be... right around here.
Still no good!
Energy readout's completely blanked!
You can't stop it?
Not that! The thing's blown past max display capacity!
There's nothing in the manuals for this!
Then to hell with the manual! I want solutions, dammit!
No, no, no, the entire facility's energy supply lines should be cut by now, all of them...
This energy... where the heck it coming from?
So you're telling me, this energy is enough to turn us to ash with just one lick, but it's just coming out of thin air?
Uh, the burden is on you to prove that hypothesis...
Shut up, and keep giving me solutions, or I'll hurl you straight in there!
Let me just give it another once-over, pipelines... switches... energy nodes...
Is this... Is this son of a bitch gonna explode on us?
The manual says... uh, I think, in general terms, current accumulated energy is now beyond the structure's theoretical safe capacity.
If it explodes, what happens?
I... I don't know...
Hey... Okay. Run me some analysis.
If something does go wrong... will it hit Yorktown? That's not far from here!
Y—Yorktown? I'm not sure...
Tell me how big it'll blow, for Pete's sake!
I told my brother-in-law it was a bad idea to pioneer out near Trimounts! It's a city of headcases!
I-I'm calculating now!
To hell with this, I'm surrounded by science nuts! Crazy poindexters!
Don't any of you have even a single breaker in your brains? Every day with your million fricking amazing projects and you never just, uh, WONDER what happens if you screw up?
The minds behind Trimountian science! And you build this giant bomb that activates itself and you literally can't even shut it down?!
I wasn't on this project! I got temp assigned to work on this by your colonel!
I—I would've definitely tested this thing a few more times myself...
Zip it! I know you people too well. One little teensy-weensy hint of success and it's like you all go goddamn blind!
But... wasn't this project led by the DOD?
Okay, then they're ALL insane! I warned my meemaw when she said her folks were coming to pioneer in Columbia! Nation built of goddamn nutcases!
Dammit, are you done calculating?! Shit, this won't work, I need to make a call...
Why can't I dial out?
Energy concentrations are too high, probably, uh, thus impacting signal transmission...
Dammit! God—DAMMIT!
Is there anywhere with better signal? Some other corridor?
This has all fallen into chaos.
Energy transmission... can't be halted? Just what is Kristen trying to do?
Mmm...
Transmitter response is still growing stronger...
It should be that the Transmitter Professor Parvis tuned is all aboard H.A.M.H.R.R., so why is there such a strong reaction here too?
Ugh... I have to...
Is there a passage anywhere? I need to keep heading down.
*sigh*. Silence.
So you came. You chose this path to move forward.
Whenever Parvis recruited for his places, he'd always tell me that the trait he most valued was a researcher's perseverance for 'progress.'
No wonder he graded you well.
What... What could be on your mind right now?
Kristen.

opts: Kristen, she've kept her secrets so very well.

lines.x: For the past while, I investigated several former Trimounts locations surveyed by Rhine Lab, until finally I found this place.
And the reality was little removed from what I imagined.
Kal'tsit gives the rock face beside her a few knocks.
We're separated from the 'Horizon Arc Project' S.H.A.F.T. by only a few hundred meters. I'm afraid Kristen has the way laid out there too, should you want to go sightseeing.
...Dr. Doctor.
I presume you've gathered that there is much more driving Rhodes Island to intervene so actively in the Trimounts affair, beyond simply who and what you've seen.
Silence, Ifrit and Saria, Loken and Rosmontis—even the revenant calling himself the Tin Man. All of them are simply ordinary people, far separated from the truth.

With regards to the Rhine Lab commotion and Kristen going out of control, the military and the Maylander Foundation are like weights once again placed on the scales, trying to preserve the semblance of a civilized nation.
But none of that is what matters most, what should concern most.
From the very beginning, Kristen has been brushing against something far beyond anyone's imaginations, and with that as a focus, has launched a charge against the sky.
I worry her actions will bring unexpected things to bear upon Terra. My intent was to stop her before then... but we were one step too slow.
On Terra today, interfering in such things is no longer easy. Civilization is much more expansive than I thought.
There'll come a day when people step far enough again.

opts: Why not share what you know with anyone else?
Isn't Kristen just trying to break through the sky?

lines.x: It's not as if I haven't before, but the end result is never ideal.
You may suggest those aboard Rhodes Island today, Amiya, Closure, the hunters with ties to Ægir... They know a great deal already, which has helped to resolve their own issues.
But when you plough the fields, you never pay mind to why the horizons form as clouds, rain and lightning, yet perhaps you may want to know when the next storm will come.

The arrogant acts of noble politicians versus repeated Catastrophe, the forecasted yield of crops versus new tax levies, these are things that ground life. The reality is, countless have no regard for even that much.
They simply do not care for the fates of others.
From a Terran's point of view, even if they did choose to believe such far-flung knowledge and information, what could they do about it? What use would it be?
The wise that take note of all around them, those bare few, are the only ones able to maintain their thought.

And the rest, when faced with things that outstrip their comprehension, more than often opt to laugh it off or persecute it, so as to protect their comfortable bubbles of ignorance from any outside challenge.
They are no more than 'lamentable,' but the truth grows ever further because of them.
'Breakthrough.' A neutral descriptor, but you still unconsciously believe that the 'sky' is something that should have no limit.
To any scholar across the land, all that happens high in the sky is unsolved natural phenomena, just as Catastrophe and the ocean are.

They will puzzle, probe at its inner workings, use their knowledge and philosophies to dissect it all—they will research all the phenomena of the sky, never considering that 'the sky should not be this way.'
Countless children have flown for it in their dreams, and they too quickly realize, the sky is not something that can be freely soared through.
Drones and vehicle altitudes have been rigorously restricted, and the spreading of every fowlbeast's wings is constrained by instinct.
This is the state of common sense upon this land. No one has ever questioned the rising of the sun and setting of the moon, and no one has ever questioned how it is all that all beings come to grow.
But Kristen has steadfastly chosen 'not to believe,' just as poets do not believe that dreams are fiction.

opts: You've been pushed to explain to me before.
I'm starting to wonder just what's ahead of us.

lines.x: Just promise me one thing, Doctor.

opts: As if I have a choice?

lines.x: No matter what it is you find next, and think next, do not let it shake you. I am by your side.
If you choose to... No. No, you won't.
I trust the you that accompanied Amiya and Rhodes Island to today, and I trust that you're prepared in your own way.
Despite how inescapably scarce our remaining time is.
—Wait.
Fall back, Doctor. What I just said will take some explaining.
You follow Kal'tsit's gaze far, to where a dangerous silver glint perches atop the rock.
A woman clings to the stalactite wall with nothing but her tail, almost like she's floating in the air, taking in the two of you with great amusement.
You feel uneasy.
It isn't Ho'olheyak's sudden appearance before you, rather that you realize how Kal'tsit, of prudence comparable to an automated machine, has made a mistake so rudimentary, even to you.
Ho'olheyak gently lands beside the two of you. Her mirthful smile draws scratches across your beating heart.
I thought you were more cautious than that, Rhodes Island physician.
But to be fair, mm-hmm... I'm surprised you two managed to find this place. Kristen never even told me its exact location.
K'uk'ulkan.
Betraying the Maylander Foundation is tantamount to funeralizing your future in Columbia. What Kristen promised must be far more generous than what Maylander could give you.
You and your clan chose a path of no return. And you're trying to find the source of your race's power from Control and Loken's research.

I advise you to stop. This is not pursuit of the historical self, it is simply self-destruction. A vast time has elapsed in which the Elders have lost their legendary powers, and now compared to the remaining Ancient races, have no—
Kal'tsit's speech is interrupted.
Ho'olheyak's tail has quietly pressed against your throat.
Yes. There's a sort of pressure your calm expression and tone of voice wreak. One that makes your database of a brain capable of turning words into weapons.
But you know that sometimes... speechcraft just doesn't work.
The Sarcophagus from Loken and the files Kristen got from who-knows-where are enough to round off this dream we've had for a long time, a dream filled to bursting with power and might.
Look at this now... look at me. My plumage, my flesh, my tail. Memory tells me how it's all begotten by my blood, a high blood by any other name. The K'uk'ulkan blood.
I'm very strong, aren't I, 'Doctor?'

opts: Please loosen your tail when you speak.

lines.x: Hahah... I don't mind your composure one bit. But, you see, if I felt like snapping your neck, then it wouldn't take much effort to do it.
I remember it happening just like this before. Without all that fantastical equipment, Kristen's hardly stronger than the average person, and I could've just as easily throttled her.
But she downplayed, minimized. Rejected 'power.'
I don't really like the oddball scientist type, but she made me realize—outside of my lineage, what do I even have?
It's a cruel irony. The words she says cling to you. She humiliated the calling and the memories I succeeded. I wanted so badly to return the favor—
—but she seemed so impossible to strike at. The placidity, like she'd gotten everything life had to offer.
So placid, it made me... envious. Made me want to claw myself apart, it shook me so badly.
She... won you over?
What is it you want?
I want to see how she ends, to guess her true intentions, to unearth her legacy. And then, I'll snatch away every last little thing that made up her mind when she ridiculed me.
Maybe I'll find the answer, just like that. Before I return to my longed-for age of the K'uk'ulkan's unrivaled dominance, I want to know, in our modern sense, what worth that has—
—what worth I have.
That's what I, Ho'olheyak, want to do. I hope you won't get in the way, 'Doctor,' Dame Kal'tsit.
Funny face you've got there.
...Let the Doctor go.
You can go seek the thing you want, if that really will resolve your misgivings.
...Mm-hmm...
...What? Just like that?
We pledge we will not respond in any way to your actions.
With the way Saria and the Tin Man act around you, I thought you were going to be the dangerous one. What's wrong?
Is that real feebleness? Because you don't carry yourself like someone without the power to resist.
I leave it to your imagination.
But you can understand that, for the Doctor's safety, I'm not going to take risks.
So it seems.
Well, I've burned my bridges with the Maylander Foundation, so the less enemies, the better, really... Kristen's plans are in motion already. I'd say we're short on free time too.
We'll leave it there, then, before the District starts responding adversely—
What?
...*sigh* Quite right. It won't do for the District to be in true, naked danger. We need to accelerate our gain of the situation.
And given you betrayed the Maylander Foundation, we really shouldn't let an outsider settle this for us. That means you, Dame Kal'tsit.
You're not that stupid. You indulged Ho'olheyak intentionally, looking to seize your moment to act.
Oh, so you guessed I'd pursue our traitor? How nostalgic, to be exploited by the Dame once again.

Intentional indulgence is giving us too much credit. The agents we assigned to be by Kristen's side defected one after another. I couldn't help but start doubting myself... and introspection is a thing that takes good time.
As for the lives of the two of you, Madame, haven't you been too careless? If you unleashed your jet-black vassal, surely restraining her wouldn't have been difficult.
'This' is not a place in which I can use any weapon.
We are already 'on top of it.'
That statement of yours is enough to draw considerable attention from the Maylander Foundation, Dame Kal'tsit. Unless you're mystifying us on purpose.
Honestly... what a surprise, Tin Man. How many bodies do you have?
Didn't I toss your poor metal skull in the trash at Loken's?
Are you letting me go this time? Or is this the deal—I kill you again, and then the Maylander Foundation lets me walk free?
(Ancient Sarkaz language) We'll talk again in a bit, traitor.
(Has my vision gone dark? He did something to my eyes—no, it's something mental—I can't move my limbs—)
It seems Arts still work here.
Originium Arts are not within the scope of the 'weaponry' sensors.
Furthermore, even the full powers of a revenant would be unable to damage their critical components in the slightest.
Then what about the Originium bomb and Arts Unit in my pockets?
Too primitive.
But these are state-of-the-art Columbian technology. They could get us buried in the deep of this land with ease.
So, anything else you haven't told the Maylander Foundation?
Are we not able to come together on this, not even with my being a Columbian, or a Sarkaz?
All throughout, I have only wanted the Doctor alone to reach this place.
The Control of Rhine Lab was a mishap in that respect.
Regretfully, you don't make the rules here. Beyond my duty to the Maylander Foundation, I have a heavy interest in this ancient ancestral home of a land.
There may be leads on Kazdel in there. I'm afraid I won't so easily—
—Speaking. Yes, Ho'olheyak has been apprehended via Arts, she's ready to... what?
The President himself?
Hah... hah... looks like your Arts aren't all that secure, my dear employer.
Oh. It seems I've underestimated Ho'olheyak. Roger that, I will of course obey.
See you later.
Isn't it strange, hm? How you can break free of such witchcraft by brute force? Your average campaign knight a century ago would've been frozen to the spot.
That voice just now, it was a Sarkaz... Witchcraft? You... what race even are you?
I ran my checks...
Always race on the mind, Ho'olheyak. You really are pitiful.
What puzzles me more, honestly... I mean, look at me, at the way I am. How could you take me for some ordinary person, killed by tossing his head away?
Yes, you have a point. So, could you put yourself back together if I crushed you?
I'll deal with Ho'olheyak. You two go on ahead.
The President has given me personal orders to unconditionally assist this Rhodes Island party to these coordinates. How very strange... I wonder when he got such a clear grasp of things...
So what of you as a Sarkaz?
The young Sarkaz detest you, and lovingly respect you too, for a great many reasons. But as I see it, your fight for the Sarkaz will go on longer still.
And of course, it will 'merely' be so.
Now, hurry up. When later comes, I hope you'll tell me everything you're willing to.
I promise that to you.
Let's go, Doctor.
I'm impressed how easily you give up on your greed. Doesn't the truth matter to you?
Don't you want to follow them down there and take in that 'ancient ancestral home' of yours?

Growing attached to your mission target on a mission, and then betraying your country with intent to misappropriate vital technology, without scruple? Not educating my subordinates properly is my disgrace.
Your damages to my body cannot rouse anger in me, not in the midst of my excessively long time undisturbed.
But as your boss and an authority of the Maylander Foundation, I suggest you hand yourself in, and try to plead a lenient sentence.
Grr, you old monster... How much have you lost over the course of your life? Does the idea of duty really still do something for you?
I once used quite the amount of Arts as a means to avert the trauma too long a time would inflict on my soul, but it wasn't entirely effective.
If you look purely at results, I'm a very conscientious one. Death will attest to that in my place.
No. I'll defeat you. I'll get everything I want.
I'll prove I'm my own self, and if that means I have to kill you, kill everyone in the Maylander Foundation, and even... be killed by you, then I'll give each and every bit of it my shot.
Heh. So this is how fighting for yourself makes your blood pound... Why did I ever put restoring my old blood first?
How many generations' worth of memories have you inherited, Ho'olheyak?
Three centuries? Or no, maybe five?
It's long enough. Long enough for a city to be rebuilt anew and destroyed again, or for a dynasty to go from rise to decline.
But to me... it is no more than the time needed to rouse myself from a nightmare.
Big words for you...
No. The reality is—
Your stretch of distress and your heartrending family history wouldn't stand up to the tale of a single scratch on my alloy fingers, young beast cub.
Ho'olheyak feels her heart stall for an instant.
Her sight blacks out again. With experience, she quickly abandons her vision, and attempts to wrap her body in Arts, to extend her limbs' sense of touch.
Even among Maylander's seasoned agents, she was eminently gifted. The K'uk'ulkan's memories are not solely a burden; they make her a natural-born, highly veteran warrior.
Every part of her body is a lethal weapon, the clarity of her mind has no equal, and her Arts would redefine guile itself.
But this time...
This time, Ho'olheyak begins to feel a hint of fear creeping up.
A prickling, sinking feeling that starts from her eardrums, and bores into her brain. She covers her ears in pain, despite the pure silence about her.
The cavern scenery begins to change, a deeper darkness diffusing out from beneath the Tin Man's feet.
(Ancient Sarkaz language) Given you want to delve into history, I'll have to share a little more of it with you.
Ho'olheyak can feel it. Her cheeks, abraded by coarse sandstorms.
Her shoulders give a faint shudder, as if bearing some colossal mass.
(Ancient Sarkaz language) You made too much light of the weight of history, K'uk'ulkan.
(Ancient Sarkaz language) You've yet to understand what it means for a memory to be graven.
7:59 P.M., H.A.M.H.R.R. altitude 5000 meters, ascent interrupted
You said you knew how to pilot this thing!
Hold on tight.
I—ungh—!
I'm trying the hardest I can, Ferdinand. If I wasn't, we would be dead.
Saria! Check the window!
The engine is on fire!
Power loss... no huge concern.
The thing looks ready to explode! What do you mean, no huge concern?!
I had no idea you could be so conversational.
Hmph. There's no end to these drones.
Ferdinand, get over here.
Grip onto this bar. No letting it go. Push it up.
See these dials? Wait until they pass this line. Then, call for me.
What are you—
Strap in. Don't let your hands leave that thing. I'm opening the hatch and heading out one moment.
Opening the hatch? What do you mean, heading out?
In midair?!
Saria, Saria! This whole thing's shaking!
Dammit, I knew I should've called my lawyer before I got onboard.
You think you can afford that lawyer without a job?
Stay calm, watch the dials. Period.
Wh—What the hell are you doing?!
I think it's—on the verge of haywire! There have been lights flashing!
SARIA! ARE YOU DONE YET?!
*huff*.
I'll take the controls. Let go of the bar.
Let go of the bar, Ferdinand.
*cough*, *cough* *cough*...
Did the firefight die down? What did you do?
I haven't seen you looking like that for years.
The pulse device was reverse-engineered from Raythean Industries tech.
I was intending for it to hijack transport drones. I didn't expect its time would come now.
Ha, haha.
Status check. Prepare to dock.
Under Saria's control, the cabin doors open up, fierce gales of wind and ice-cold air pouring straight into the aircraft.
Hrrrfff—I should've worn a few more layers.
Put on your mask. It'll make that a little bit easier for you.
If I'm honest, I thought you'd jump straight out like some down-on-her-lucker and leave a fresh hole in the lab ceiling.
But like an academic, you pulsed the drones first to stop them nearing this craft, and then completed a proficient midair docking.
Nasti was meticulous in building this lab. I don't think she would have zero precautions against me.
Beside which, I couldn't guarantee you'd withstand atmospheric pressures at this altitude.
It looks like you have been busy since you left Defense.
What have you been doing all these years?
Work.
Hmph. Work.
Don't undo your seatbelt, Ferdinand.
If you want to pull Rhine Lab back from the brink, then show me how you do it.
I'm not your Section underling, Saria, and you're not Defense's Director anymore.
Don't talk to me that way.
Can you stand?
Yes! I'm fine!
...But you'll have to keep me steady!
This sensation is bizarre... Are we really in midair?
The 'starpod' is practically on top of our heads...
We're still far, Ferdinand. The aircraft read 5037 meters on the dial just now. The barrier's still another kilometer from where we are.
You don't seem moved at all.
Hmph. I suppose so. Learning to pilot in your college days...
The army still thinks this is some kind of superweapon. This equipment, these monitor devices and readouts... Nasti built this to be the best lab in all of Trimounts.
Or perhaps this was just the first step of some grand experiment?
That devil... just how much did she hide from me? I never thought her participation went that deep.
Hold on, Saria. I see a military transporter. They got here faster than we anticipated.
They must be nearby. I assume they'll finish boarding before long. We need to hurry before they take over this place fully...
Saria?
The man twists his head around, and finds the ex-Director of the Defense Section has already headed deep down the toroidal corridor.
Alright, alright.
Once I get back, I'm having a nice, long talk with Jara.
Preferably to double my stock options.
You follow in Kal'tsit's steps. You've rarely seen her this solemn.
You begin, secretly, to take stock of her as you walk behind her. You realize you've barely ever had the chance to see Kal'tsit in such quiet.
Your perturbance begins to turn to tension. To your recollection, Kal'tsit is only unsettled or restless once in two blue moons, and each time the aftermath has needed absolutely no description.
You move to open your mouth, to say something. Just anything.

opts: What's the Tin Man's goal here?
Did Ho'olheyak injure you at all?

lines.x: Doctor.
Perhaps I have slightly lost my composure, but it is for other reasons. I can be sure it's not that dangerous ahead of us. You can relax.
That said...
You've asked me a great many questions in the past, which I've never answered for you straight.
Today, you may well finally have your answers.
It was as early as Saria's first contact with me, on the mention of Kristen, that I'd envisaged her going to any lengths to accomplish this.
Brashly attempting a space flight carries risk nobody could anticipate, though Saria didn't yet know the full extent.
Not until the events in Trimounts, until Operators Ptilopsis and Silence were drawn into a seemingly unrelated incident—
Yet no matter when, even when I surmised she was trying to re-challenge the skies—I admit, I had no anticipation the situation would be what it is today.
With how things have unfolded, with our personally witnessing the S.H.A.F.T.'s scope and scale, H.A.M.H.R.R.'s ascent, and... the research Loken Williams is now attempting.
It was soon after, with hindsight, that I realized what Rhine Lab Control Kristen Wright is attempting is impossible to achieve. Some outside force assisted her, massively influenced her.

Borrowing military funds and connections, continuing the research her parents left behind, the young genius has used Rhine Lab as a shell to deceive everyone, and point herself directly at her one greatest, most obstinate hope in life.
She plans to shred the sky.

opts: I guessed as much, but... then what?
I can't swallow it. ALL THAT, just for her fixation.

lines.x: She's doing it solely to witness the truth of it, to make all mankind witness the truth of it.
And what happens after? A brand new perspective, a flourishing science, the moment when the sky becomes possibility itself, and even the very upheaval of how war is fought.
People could reach other cities in a tenth of the time. Air vehicles, once a valuable rarity, could become typical transport means overnight.
But Kristen has chosen to leave all this to the coming generations.
She's never considered this from the angle of benefit—be it how she personally stands to gain, or how everyone does. Even the potential crises invited have gone ignored by her.
Is there one more possibility, that Kristen, who lost her parents at a young age and grew up amid skepticism and derision, is out just to prove this one truth to people, and nothing more?
This may well be what Saria thinks. As fellow students with Kristen in days past, as the very first supporter of her dreams...
Regardless of how bleak Kristen Wright's past was, how pure her vision, even how little was of her own personal doing...
She has never had any right to avoid these responsibilities.
Think of what Operators Ifrit and Silence have been through. Rhine Lab Control is self-centered, of that there is no doubt.
Saria wishes for her to assume these responsibilities. Saria wants to urge her to turn her eyes, fixed towards the sky, down to each and every individual, living, breathing person in Rhine Lab...
Perhaps Saria was meant to be the first to see Kristen's vision through for her, but then within Rhine Lab, at the crossroads of morality and ethics, everything spiraled out of control.
Regardless of anything, Saria and I have both been a step behind.
Given Terra's current Originium technology, tearing the sky apart would require the sum electricity of all of Terra over an interminably long period.
And this is purely a mathematical comparison. Nobody could ever converge so much energy on one point, let alone have the means to usefully output it towards the sky.
But... in the core city of Chernobog, a great many things have happened, all revolving around a Sarcophagus. Do you still remember?
At that point, Ursus's shallow research was sufficient to draw a relatively impressive amount of energy from the Sarcophagus, and now... the energy is enough to realize Kristen's deranged vision.

At least she hasn't chosen to share this information with the military or the Maylander Foundation. Such a material edge would make the state apparatus lose its mind, and certainly suffice to propel it into a rapid new hegemony.

opts: How did Kristen manage to find this?

lines.x: ...Dr. Doctor.
Remember what I said. If you can, for the next while, call to mind your past with Rhodes Island.
Do not be rattled.
A violent light sweeps over your body. You feel a shred of familiarity impossible to process.
With no warning, out of all the things before you right now, you recall that scene. That scene you've never once forgotten, up to this day.
The Sarcophagus. A cold, frigid light.
Amiya grips your hand. You remember nothing. You're drawn into the maelstrom of Chernobog. It is the start of everything.
So much time has passed... but something, some sentiment even longer-lived than that fills the dense haze of your memories.
It stirs in you grief, and reminiscence, and discomfort... it is part of you, yet you reject it with all you can.
You strive to settle down your psyche. Faced with this quiet noise and dazzling darkness, this indescribable yet infinitesimally brief torment, your vision returns.
You narrowly avoid a gasp of surprise.
But this unknown structure has preempted you, and the sounds of resuscitation echo through the vast hall.
(Unknown language) ...Unfathomable.
(Unknown language) That after such... such lengths of time, I would lay eyes again... on a colleague... and an attendant.
(Unknown language) Truly... far too unfathomable...

opts: Why can I understand... your language?

lines.x: (Unknown language) Who am I?
(Standard Columbian) Who am I?
Why would you use Terran language...? Are you not aware of my existence?
What happened to you? Answer me on that one's behalf, attendant. AMa-10.
I order you to answer me, attendant.
...Dr. Doctor is an amnesiac. The Doctor is not one among those you recognize—not anymore.
The Doctor is now one of Terra. One of Rhodes Island.
And we have experienced a great deal together, up to this very day.
...Rhodes Island?
(Unknown language) Rhodes Island?
I see.
Fine, then, 'amnesiac.' So why did not you relate everything once again to this... 'Doctor'?
Ah, I see now.
You've always had your doubts about this 'Doctor's' true nature. You'd rather trust one relearning about the world, when the alternative is to recover a stretch of memories you deem unreliable.
Yes... quite understandable. None of what's happened on the ground can be forgery, and if there had been sufficient intervention, Terra wouldn't be in the state it is now.
It seems not a thing has gone as we hoped. Even if you don't remember that... Dr. Doctor.
What a rare chance this is. To meet the two of you, here. Yet under these conditions...
Well, then. 'Dr. Doctor' the amnesiac, and AMa-10 claiming the name Kal'tsit. What did you come here for?
Has Kristen put nerves in you? Has all this flown beyond your grasp?

opts: I don't think you've answered me yet.
Quit posturing at us like that!

lines.x: I am a devised personality simulation. For tens of thousands of years I have guarded this dark hall, and the ice cold bodies of hundreds of thousands of our people.
I once was our last hope, a pessimistic attempt, a synonym for nihilism.
But right now, perhaps to you insignificantly miniscule two... I am the accessory to and the invisible hand behind Kristen's mad plan.
I am the 'Preserver' with a foot in the grave. Revere all that lies before you. I am the dying of Civilization itself.
Position correct, Transmitter response... extremely strong...
I—
Director Jara...?
Why are you here?
You made it here by my blessing. Such is life.
You... came to stop me.
You haven't let go of that weapon I gave you. I'm delighted.
It doesn't seem like you're here on some hot-headed impulse, either.
Correct.
This is your very last chance, child.
Withdraw, or continue?
I will... learn to be the warrior you described, Director Jara.
Silence, you're fully aware that Kristen is not about to target any town in the land with H.A.M.H.R.R.'s received energy.
You have no need to stop her.
Like any scientist would, she is preparing to go where none have set foot before, and that's all.
Perhaps so, Director Jara.
I certainly don't know Control like you do, but if you say so, I am willing to believe it.
However, I will continue to attempt to bring a stop to all this, and I will make no exception, even if you're the one that stands in my way.
Why?
Because—whatever this experiment is, if Control freely conducts it, and succeeds in it—
And this being the Control of Rhine Lab, we all know, whether out of emotional confidence or rational judgment, that she is very likely to succeed—
—What do you think the researchers of Columbia are going to see in her?
After holding your job in Rhine Lab Human Resources Investigation for so long, you would know the people here inside and out.
...A hero.
Kristen would become a hero and an idol throughout all of Columbian science.
Yes. She'll become the scientist's paragon, the benchmark of a researcher.
And then, the floodgates will be open forever.
Item after item, project after project, researchers will be in perpetual delight.
But, Director Jara, only she is Kristen Wright. Only her.
And the others? The ones bragging about themselves as geniuses and leaders? The ones inspired by this 'pioneer spirit by any means necessary?'
The ones who'll put their own obsessions and ideals into practice, all without the slightest apprehension?
The greedy, the egotistical, the overreaching, the stubborn, the ones like us?
If they take the conduct of Rhine Lab's Control to be a virtue—
Then that—is a future I am sure you can imagine the shape of.
Perhaps we don't even need to imagine...
With the research you yourself conducted on Rhine Lab, you've never been in the dark.
I know very well, Silence.
I know very well how much of that kind of thing has happened, and is happening inside Trimounts—no, within the borders of Columbia.
There have been plenty of insane and alluring projects placed before me, their owners starry-eyed, their smiles sincere.
They wholeheartedly believe they serve 'pure science.' They're the Pioneers of the wilds that we know.

While all you and your colleagues have to do is evaluate whether inside their experimental plans, their grand blueprints, there lies even the off-chance it might incur the slightest 'cost' or 'waste.'
'Cost.'

Sometimes it comes in the form of a fertile field, and sometimes it's some number of terminal patients or death row inmates. Sometimes it's a village or a town, and sometimes it's some specific race or even nation.
And as I see it, sometimes, it's their own selves too.
Are you scared of that, Director Jara? Or do the possibilities for science's forward progress excite even you to no end?
Director Jara, never upon this earth has there existed a specimen of 'pure science' in a glass bell.
Science lies in our hands, in the people's hands. You have to know its temperature, its weight, whether it burns people, whether it will bring suffering or if it will console.
Those people forever claim that they're the way forward, but all they do is set foot with wild abandon in whichever direction they wish to.
But, Silence, that is what progress is.
Nobody knows where the path lies. This is the walk they must take.
The fortunate will find the right place, and enter the light. The misfortunate will run into dead ends, and spin in circles.
But there is no way you can make them stop.
You're a researcher yourself. You know what's most unstoppable of all.
—It is craving.
Matter can't bind it, morality can't bind it, and rules can't bind it all the same.
Across this land, all throughout history, before Kristen, there were those greedy, egotistical, overreaching, stubborn people you speak of, and when did they ever stop?
And so, from now on, they must stop.
At the very least, they must stop, before stooping to see just what sits beneath their feet.
Director Jara, I feel—scientist's fuel should not derive from cravings and ideals, nor should it come from calculations and choices.
Science is a clear, incisive look at every one of us.
Silence, you've said something very beautiful. Fit to be hung in the corridors of a university library.
But I'm afraid not one researcher is willing to see that quote on the walls of their lab.
It is lip service.
And so, I will make it practice, Director Jara.
I don't want to give up midway, and I still have your blessing.
Silence, Kristen has spent a very long time readying this plan. I don't know how much she's poured into it.
But her determination can't be any less than her parents' was.
You're—
Fine... this isn't you, it's me.
I can't even watch this. I don't want it all to melt into thin air again.
In which case, Director Jara, what is it exactly that you hope for? To see Kristen succeed, or to bring her back safe and sound?
I know a researcher who goes by Ruby. Her greatest wish is to clock out on time and retire without hassle.

I just ran across a soldier. I imagine his only wish right now is to get out of this out-of-control S.H.A.F.T. without incident, and then flee the clutches of Trimounts and its scientific loons together with his family.
Is Control's wish any more lofty, compared to theirs?
Because the thing she wants—makes sufficient 'progress?'
Or is this simply because the feelings between you, and her—are sufficiently close?
Director Jara, you know this isn't fair.
You can't be this selfish.
I... am this selfish. I can't use morality and justice to fool myself in order to let you pass.
We must concede what emotions weigh. Kristen has this dream. I can't just stand here and fold my arms.
Silence, you would understand this, wouldn't you?
But if we can stop Control, she could very well still—
She won't.
She won't, child.
I know full well... when she set this project into motion, she never planned to return.
Since long ago—even, since we first met, there has been no... taking back any of this.
Silence, it makes me very happy that you could stand in my way here. I can even say that I support your viewpoint.
But, as Kristen's...
...family, I still won't let you through.
Go on and show me. Are you really... prepared to bear every word you've said?
What you face from hereon is more than a single Parvis, or a lone Kristen.
What you face—is every person that attempts to forge forward.
I will. I must be ready. I promise you this.
A scientist must press forth, and is bound to press forth.
Perhaps, some—like Control, will be more proficient at the 'forward' portion.
But there must be someone to step up, and ensure that science—
—remains in the people's hands.
...'Preserver'...
I never thought it would truly be you.
But if it is, then how can you be...
Aiding in a little animal's mad schemes?
Or witnessing a young civilization take its first step beyond the cradle?
You came too late, 'Doctor.'
My wait... has lost all meaning.

opts: Compared to... this, Trimounts has been a drop in the bucket.

lines.x: It's you helping Kristen. She used some means to get in contact with you.
Terra's history, civilizations, languages, races, technological attainments—I have come to know it all through Columbia's networks.
Hilariously, modern surface information transmission isn't even able to cover the globe, but your methods of mutually assured destruction happen to have evolved with competent efficiency.
Terra's methods are too primal and reliant on Originium, and with such conversion rates, realizing Kristen's attempt... The energy source required would be outlandish.
It's near to impossible that Terra could have the technology to solve this enigma. Save for—
Correct.
I gave her the last of my blood. May as well supply a new heart than watch over a dying light, perhaps.
You actually, honestly—
—Why do you not regret it? All your efforts over such an immense amount of time... all gone to waste.
Yes. Gone to waste.
I shut the project down personally. The orders were input by myself.
It's a decision I cannot under any circumstances regret.
It'll be soon, now. Very soon.
A giant tremor ripples out from somewhere far away. The lights within this subterranean space give a few flickers.
This being, calling itself the 'Preserver,' falls silent for a while, then seems to let out a deep breath.
It's only an infant's curious glance—
Yet what obstructions is it tasked to overcome?
8:11 P.M., S.H.A.F.T. energy accumulation abnormal
That Rhodes Island corporation has trained you well.
Huff... hff....
Can you still lift your arms?
I'm fine.
What about yours?
Relax, I buy my health insurance.
Are we going to keep doing this?
If you're still going to block my way, yes.
Alright, let's keep at it.
Wait, something's not right.
The temperature... Even if the S.H.A.F.T.'s preparing to fire, the temperature here shouldn't have risen this high.
The army has cut off practically every power source in an effort to stop it charging.
Including every security alarm and control system? Those morons—
Quick! We have to get out of here!
There are still some troops stationed up above, Director Jara. I'm counting on you to get them away.
Where are you headed?
Power to the control systems has been cut. Why does Control still have remote operation of this place?
Transmitter response is still strengthening. I... still have a chance.
Silence!
A heatwave washes with a burn past Jara's face. The Liberi researcher she'd never paid particular mind to before now continues straight inward, running deeper into the S.H.A.F.T.'s layers.
Jara is suddenly struck by a feeling that there is nothing, not one thing, that could get in that silhouette's way.
Perhaps, she really can make good on what she promised herself.
She imagines, come that time, that maybe she can be of a little help.
Looks like prep work on the emitter has wrapped up.
'Science is a clear, incisive look at every one of us.'
Hah. I'm no scientist.
Kristen, you'll fly for outer space just like you've been dreaming of...
I'm sure of it.
Nasti, couldn't you choose someplace with a better view?
At what point can you finally stop ragging on me?
When I went to observe in Leithanien, I once saw a local fireworks show. They invited the best composers, just to write for the occasion.
As the first firework went off, all the musicians started playing in unison. That, my, my, that was a scene of beauty.
Your figures of speech are too deficient. Leithanians wouldn't like you.
True, and true. But still, we're here today in Trimounts seeing a sight that glitters just as gold.
A ring floating in the air, drone bullets drawing out raindrops of pure light, splashes of sparks off armor plating...
Just as good as a fireworks show.
Looking at the clock, it's about time for the climax of the performance.
Here comes the thrilling, stirring sforzando—
It's... prettier than I imagined. Rhine Lab ought to be charging all the city-siders enjoying the show.
It's a success. H.A.M.H.R.R.'s materials and structure have withstood the shock from the energy beam for the time being.
Next comes the charging and focus phase. As long as we can collect the readings from such high-pressure conditions...
I'm afraid at this point, the army and Maylander guys grab their last chance to stop it from fully focusing.
Aren't you worried about Control?
Like she ever needed us to worry.
We're going the same way. And that's all we are.
We can only play our own parts well, and then... pray she's lucky.
(Sarkaz language) If one day, Kazdel too hangs from the firmament, then I'll engrave your name into my stoves.
Once upon the land, there was no meaning in looking up.
There was nothing worth looking at up there, nothing worth exploring. That place was the domain of those who indulged in fantasies, those with their heads in the clouds.
But now, each and every person lifts their heads, looking up at the beam of light connecting heaven and earth.
Looking up at the once untouchable sky.
Among the stars, within the artificial constructs that appear so small before the torrent of light, brews the human ambition of this current epoch.
Kristen, Kristen...
Sure enough, you set this plan into motion a long time ago. Everything you did for the DOD was a fancy show.
Hah! What's the principle behind all this? The efficiency and stability of this high-energy confinement field... I've never heard of anything like that before.
If I recall correctly, you boasted that you were the foremost expert in high energy physics when you joined Rhine.
...One of the foremost experts.
My God...
You should thank your lucky stars, Saria, that there's nobody like Kristen in your field.
It's just not fair. And yet she... she doesn't even bother to publish these results.
The H.A.M.H.R.R. is currently charging.
According to the military's Horizon Arc Project, once the charging phase is complete, all of this energy will be focused and undergo excitation a second time to deliver powerful wide-area strikes.
Sure. How much longer will it take?
Kristen turned the project dossiers into scrap paper... All I can say is, it won't be long now.
This is our last chance to stop it.
As well as our last chance to stop her.
What are you still standing around slack-jawed for, Ferdinand? Time isn't on our side.
Saria, take a look at the densitometer over there.
The needle seems to be fluctuating a lot.
No, I'm not talking about the needle. The wobbling is a normal phenomenon of high-energy confinement fields. I'm talking about... the unit of measurement.
You can take a guess how many times larger this unit is than the one on the instruments in my lab.
The energy it absorbed just now is enough to power every single one of Columbia's nomadic cities for at least six months.
And it's showing no signs of stopping.
And we're now in the center of the laboratory carrying this tremendous amount of energy.
Even if Nasti is a genius, she can't create a receptacle that stores energy in amounts beyond the realm of cognition.
In other words–
No matter what direction it ultimately focuses the energy in, once the second-degree excitation begins, this lab won't be safe from the consequences.
Her end goal has never changed.
If her ultimate goal is the sky, and the sky can only be contested with this amount of energy...
What exactly is up there?
That's none of my concern.
All I know is, this is a reckless experiment.
And it's doomed to fail.

Saria, there's nothing stopping you from packing your bags, finding a nice hillside with a great view, and enjoying a drink while you watch this fireworks show that may well go down in the annals of Columbian history.
That isn't going to happen.
What about you? You were never the risk-taking type.
Me?
Looks like my old friends are coming, and they'll be here sooner than expected.
You're right though. I'm not taking any chances. The technical manual has the location of the escape pod, and I know where it is.
I'm not particularly interested in seeing the one who fired me, and it's not like you need my help.
An opportunity to document high energy physics phenomena of this magnitude is rare, and I don't intend to pass it up. Maybe you'll see a new paper from me in the journals next quarter.
I promise, it'll be era-defining.
Unlike Kristen, I don't tie all my intellect to my personal grievances.
The fact that she's consigning all this knowledge to the grave, knowledge that could transform Terra's entire academic landscape... That's something I can't abide.
Do what you need to do, Saria. We don't have much time.
Stay safe.
Ferdinand.
It's been a while, Colonel.
I'm not surprised you survived, but if I were in your shoes, I'd pretend to be dead.
Get the hell out of Dodge. Go to Victoria or Leithanien. With your skills, you can always pick up a teaching job.
I didn't expect to hear those words coming from you.
Let me say this one more time, Ferdinand: It's over.
I've already spoken with the Vice President. The DOD's defeat is absolute.
As for whether or not this whole situation can be salvaged, or who controls my fate, I actually don't care that much anymore.
Let's save some dignity for each other, Ferdinand. Get out of here, and I'll save a crossbow bolt for myself.
Huh, what was that? Sorry, my mind wandered for a second there.
Also, you're blocking the radiometer display.
This S.H.A.F.T. here runs a lot deeper than I expected...
I'm afraid the military simply failed to fully understand its structure.
Right here... is where the Transmitter's response is the strongest.
You...
What are you doing here, Silence?
Professor... Parvis...
Silence... The location of this lab is supposed to be top secret. How did you manage to find your way here?
Silence tries hard to open her eyes wide, discerning the things in the room.
In the corner of the room, an instrument is humming as if breathing. A Transmitter sits in a transparent tank, bobbing up and down. Across the room are several books with black covers.
As if sensing Silence's presence, the Transmitter increases its activity. A strong wave of dizziness makes Silence furrow her brow and shake her head a few times.
Ah, that's right. You have your own Transmitter...
Hmm, these reactive tendencies... You injected yourself with the Transmitter? Though, I'm not surprised.
Does it cause you occasional discomfort?
An entirely normal response. I also have it inside my body. It can be quite overwhelming for an old man like myself, but it shouldn't be a problem for you youths.
Still, this is the only way to better control the reaction process.
Don't worry. Once this experiment is over, the long-distance intertwining properties of this batch of Transmitter will be lost, and they won't affect the long-term health of the injected.
What experiment? You're conducting an experiment here?
Silence, did you hurt your hand?
Does it get in the way of your work?
I'm fine.
Then I won't ask any more questions. Now is the most critical moment.
That you've found your way here... might just be the help I've needed.
I was just complaining how much easier the data processing would be if you were here.
Lately, I... Actually, never mind.
Tell me, what kind of experiment are you running?
There has only ever been one area of research I've fully dedicated my energy to.
...Chimerism.
Precisely. This is the last question to be answered in chimera research.
But, this is...
Professor, what are all these documents? The text on them seems...
Quite 'evil,' right? This is an ancient Sarkaz tome that records the 'history' of a certain ethnic branch.
That Sarkaz branch not only enjoys long lifespans, but can even transcend the shackles of physical form and exist in the world in a purely spiritual state.
They have been dubbed, the 'revenants.' The pronunciation can be a bit esoteric, but I'm sure you'll be able to find some records in Victorian.
Silence, have you ever considered what chimerism does at the level of the soul?
A soul chimera?
These 'revenants,' the so-called 'soul' in ancient Originium Arts and Sarkaz witchcraft, can they be produced a posteriori?
You...
Are you trying to create an artificial consciousness?
You've always been my finest student.
This is something I've been working on for decades, to little avail. That is, until Ferdinand told me that he and Dorothy had jointly produced this particular 'Transmitter.'
You can't begin to imagine how excited I was, Silence!
