I was absolutely sure that the properties of the 'Transmitter'... were sufficient to form the soil from which artificial consciousness could be born.
However, in this land, disturbances will always exist no matter how sophisticated the lab is. These disturbances have plagued me for the longest time...

There are so many possibilities. Perhaps the energy used to excite the Transmitter was insufficient, perhaps the laboratory was not sealed off enough, perhaps Originium interferes with the generation and aggregation of consciousness.
But, none of that matters anymore. All mysteries will finally be unraveled today.
This will be my final research project.
The most optimal research environment is about to take shape.
Professor Parvis, you were responsible for using Transmitter to solve Arc-01's accuracy control problem. This is... the new Transmitter core.

Kristen wanted to do something she saw as taking a risk. She thought her work to be meaningful... I hate to be a critic of my peer's efforts, but let's be honest: we researchers have been cowards for far too long.
Mediocrity, boredom, risk-aversion, indecisiveness... Silence, those of us with the knowledge and ability have to do more than just 'explore' or 'progress.'
We carry science upon our shoulders, are commanded by it, are driven by it. It requires us to take on responsibilities that only a strong soul can endure.
––Usher in a new era.
...'Usher in a new era'?
Silence... You deserve to be here with me to witness this crowning achievement.
Soon, very soon now... the dark ages of mediocrity will come to an end, and the pantheon of science must return.
You're shaking all over. I've rarely seen you like this.
Is that so? Perhaps the Transmitter's reaction is getting a bit too intense. Maybe I really am getting too old.
I'm sure you can relate with me, Silence.
Yes, I can.
I can see you've really been looking forward to this moment.
Of course.
Then, there's one thing I have to make clear. I will destroy this Transmitter core.
What...?
I mean, I'm putting a stop to everything that's happening here.
Whether it's Control's plan or just your personal ambitions, all of this has to be reexamined.
That's what I'm here for.
That girl shook your allegiance to science, but I've always believed that you had the makings of a researcher.
And as such, I never really blamed you.
But this time, whether or not this is some ridiculous joke, I'm not going to stand for it.
I'll have you apologize to me, Silence.
I won't take back a single word I said, Professor.
I don't think I've done anything wrong, be it back then or now. What you people are really after is wanton power.
That's absurd. You couldn't be more wrong, Silence.
That's right. Say what you will about me craving power. Because I know what kind of research is important, and what kind of research can shape the future.
What, are you going to regurgitate that old platitude about science belonging to the masses?
No!
Let me tell you, Silence, that science belongs to the Übermenschen. The supermen.
The mediocre can go elect their politicians and kiss the feet of their betters...
But only science is above their meddling.
What about you then? Are you part of the 'Übermenschen' you aspire to?
...That ship has already sailed for me.
But if the consciousness I create is rational enough, grand enough, then it can––
It can't, Professor.
If you don't even have faith in humanity, what makes you think that this nascent consciousness can supersede everything?
You don't understand.
You've poured your heart and soul into the field of chimeric research, attempting time after time to craft powerful creations through bioengineering. Is that all because...
You can't run from your own weakness?
...Silence, I won't allow you to throw baseless speculation around like that.
This is... my last hope to break free from mediocrity.
...I didn't expect to see you here.
My former Defense colleagues.
That's right, Director Saria. 'Former colleagues.'
We are now employees of Components Control Section.
'Components Control.'
You aren't allowed past this point.
You're doing a better job than last time.
I appreciate the sentiment.
You all know what Kristen is trying to do. All this accomplishes is–
I don't know, and I don't particularly care.
You know my ID number from Rhine, Saria.
011. That was before Jara came. Kristen and I interviewed you, James.
Back then, I had offers from other companies, including Volvort Kochinski and Beachbrella. But, I didn't give them a second thought.
Because I always believed in you. You and Kristen. I believed you two were different from the rest of them.
That was just your ego speaking, James. This whole place is about to crash. I'd recommend you hurry up and grab an escape pod.
I'll wait here until the moment we crash.
But before that, want to see something spectacular, Saria?
Nasti described it to us...
Saria notices the entire corridor shifting.
Is that—
I've never seen the land from this high up before.
The H.A.M.H.R.R. has begun altitude adjustment.
Out of the way! There's no time!
Dammit, what's going on?! This blasted thing's spinning?
If I had to guess, this would be the 'altitude adjustment' mentioned in the technical manual, which is the step right before secondary excitation.
Sir, do you need my help?
Why are these doors in the corridor shut? Where's Ferdinand?
He slipped away just now–
Go after him!
Are there no defense systems here other than the partition doors in the corridor?
If I had any say in it, this place would be stuffed full of power armor.
Freeze, Ferdinand.
Got you.
I need you to leave me alone, Colonel. Every minute here is precious.
And dealing with you would waste all my energy.
I thought we were peas in a pod, Ferdinand. Sober rationalists who would weigh the pros and cons before making a decision.
Looks like I had the wrong read on you.
Come with us.
The beam excitation reading is 1.22. A constant should be introduced here to renormalize the entire formula...
What the hell are you talking about?
I'm laughing at you, Blake.
I'm laughing at how pathetic you are. Your power, your medals, your rank in the DOD... You never lost sight of those.

But now, we're standing at an altitude that few have ever touched, surrounded on all sides by scientific miracles. Just by reaching our hands out, we're on the cusp of plucking new formulae from these instruments.
Take a good look. Here, all the honors and accolades you've clung to are so small, so insignificant.
And what about your Rhine Lab, Ferdinand?
Haven't you been fixated on that?
Let me make a correction. I've been fixated on what Rhine Lab represents.
Rhine Lab is my toolbox. My ladder, my arm. But it is not my purpose.
In case you've forgotten, I can proudly proclaim that I am one of the finest high energy physicists in the land... No, in both the land and the skies.
What I've been fixated on has always been science itself.
Kristen, Kristen...
She created all of this, and let it go to waste. A truly prodigal genius she is.
And I envy her––
But I also have to admit, it's been an incredible ride.
And so, each and every second of my time is insanely valuable, and I can't afford to waste a moment on you.
Listen to me, Ferdinand. Don't forget who you take orders from, and who gave you your new lease on life!
Colonel, do you know how to stop a curious scientist?
You either satisfy him, or you kill him.
Alright.
Get it over with. Don't waste time.
One last thing I'd like to remind you of, soldiers.
The H.A.M.H.R.R. will crash after reactivation, taking all of us with it.
All this data might mean nothing to you, but... your lives, I trust, are very important.
If you want to know where the escape pods are, you shouldn't be in such a hurry.
As for you, Blake, I'd save an extra bolt... Either for yourself, or the one you answer to. Doesn't matter to me.
Just, don't break my instruments.
You...
I... I've been memorizing the basic structure of the central nervous system lately, over and over again, from top to bottom.
Isn't that one of the assignments they give you in an intro biology course?
Yes, it's one of the absolute fundamentals. If I were to ask this question to any of my students, should they be stumped for even a second, I would drop their grade down to a C.
But... these days, I haven't been able to recite it from memory, not even a single time.
I keep stopping partway, I keep forgetting. I keep starting from the top, going around in circles.
Every morning, I sit from five to seven at the breakfast restaurant across from Rhine Headquarters, and I give it my best shot.
My nerves are deteriorating. Everything I've studied my whole life is now abandoning me in this moment.
Maybe in five, or even three years' time, I'll become a demented old man who can only bask in the sun, having forgotten all the pride and fulfillment I've enjoyed over the past fifty-eight years.
Silence, I refuse to go out like this.
...Professor...
My fate is already sealed.
Now, get out of my lab! Don't you dare touch it!
No.

You speak of 'Übermenschen' and 'strong souls'... People like that might actually exist; people capable of transcending time, limitations, and this very land itself. But, Professor, you are no such man.
Your words are of the weak, 'mediocre masses' you so ridicule.
They are the desires of a 'human.'
Transcending time, limitations, and this land itself?
In the myths of Minos and Kjerag, gods are the bestowers, masters, and deciders of all things in creation.
But science is by no means a god. It is just a path for people to traverse. It needs to be examined from time to time, repaired from time to time, and reined in from time to time.
The fruits it bears should not be the grandiose vision of megalomaniacs, but precisely the 'mediocre expectations of the masses.'
No... No! Get away from it, Silence!
It's over, Professor.
What you need is rest and recovery. After this, I'll take you to the hospital.
The Transmitter hub has been destroyed.
Be it your experiment or Kristen's plans, without the Transmitter, there shouldn't be–
...Haha.
Hah, transcending this very land...
Yes, perhaps... I made another tremendous achievement that even I did not notice.
I just happened upon it, without investing much time into it. As a matter of fact, it didn't even arise from one of my own projects.
But, that doesn't matter, does it?
Of course, of course. Why hadn't I noticed?
We've all been drawn in by her... From the very beginning, we've always walked the same path.

The old man stares blankly at the display panel. On the monitoring screen, the giant manmade ring suspended atop the sky's zenith is illuminated by the beam of energy, gathering within it a majestic platinum.
Perhaps because the Transmitter hub was destroyed, or perhaps because the scouring of the beam was too intense, the H.A.M.H.R.R. starts to shake slightly.
Urgh...
Why... has the Transmitter signal not stopped?
Let me ask you, Silence, if you have no way of becoming a leader, then what shame is there in being a follower?
If I admit my weakness and indecisiveness, then, it is a perfectly valid option for me to follow a truly strong soul, is it not?
This was meant to be my backup plan, but now...
What... are you doing...?
I've suffered mediocrity for too long. I must...
I can still catch up to her. She, too, must shine.
All my hard work, all of my pursuits... must have meaning.
At least, this is much better... than becoming a vegetative old man.
The energy density is already sufficient, as long as...
Professor! Professor! Parvis, what are you doing?!
Silence feels the Transmitter's influence on her fading; at the same time, the consciousness of the elderly Caprinae in front of her is rapidly dissipating.
When the transcendent one sets out on her journey, I do hope... that on her long, long path, she won't mind being followed... by a timid hitchhiker.

At least, I can become a part...
Silence, look–
Are you merging your consciousness with the Transmitter? To make up for the hub's destruction? No––!
Drat, these data... What's going to happen if the phases are thrown completely out of order?
How do I stop these responses?! Professor! Parvis!
This is completely futile! Your consciousness can't be preserved within the Transmitter!
This transmission will only destroy your nervous system–
A faint sigh, perhaps a slight chuckle.
Parvis's body has become an empty shell, no longer host to any conscious will. All his attachments, all his cowardice, dissipated without a trace.
Professor?
Within the monitor, the trembling of the H.A.M.H.R.R. seems to gradually calm down.
He completed his experiment, in a most unexpected way.
How could this...?
Silence! Silence!
Silence, are you in here?
So you really were here!
Step back! I'm coming in!
I finally found you! You had me scared to death, Silence!
When I saw you snatch the car keys off those soldiers and floor it outta there, I knew you were about to do something really, really dangerous!
This place is crazy scary! Not only is this place crawling with soldiers up above, but I even got a real jumpscare from that exploding energy beam!
Ifrit, why are you...?
To save you of course, duh! Didja know I learned how to drive? A real shame you didn't get to see my mad skills!
With my driving skills, I might even get bumped up to elite op! I...
What's wrong, Silence?
Why's Parvis here? He... Why isn't he moving?
He... I don't know.
In the end, he achieved his goal. On the other hand, I... haven't really achieved anything.
I was thinking...
Had I been more decisive, had I been more determined...
Ifrit, how am I supposed to stop people like them – such powerful, brightly-shining souls?
I... Will I never... be able to reach Saria or Kristen?
Stop jokin' around already, Silence!
Ifrit frenetically grabs Silence by the collar.
This isn't the Silence she knows.
You're the strongest woman I've ever seen!
Rosmontis is pretty powerful, and so is Blaze. All of Rhodes Island's operators have their own special skills.
But, you're the best out of all of 'em, Silence! It's because you... You've done so much for me. So much that I, I... to be honest, I don't even know where to start.
I pushed through everything. You and Saria both, you both overcame it all!
I know... what you're trying to do is really huge and really complicated.
The guys you're trying to stop might also be really powerful, and really hard to deal with.
So, that's why you should let more people help you! They have so many helping them out, so should you have to fight all by yourself?
Obviously... You have me, and Saria too. We're here by your side!
Silence looks at the kid in front of her.
Compared to three years ago, her complexion is much more robust. Her body is also stronger, and her entire being brims with vitality.
The girl's health is the product of her own efforts.
It's just... when did she get so tall?
You're right.
I need to get ready. I have to keep going.
And you. It's because of you.
The S.H.A.F.T.'s output phase may be nearing completion, and it won't be safe in this building.
Come, Ifrit. Let's get out of here.
Also, someone's waiting for us to pick her up.
As rudimentary a toy as it may be, the spear of a secondary civilization breaking through the sky for the first time was enough to render this nascent world dumbfounded.
Dr. Doctor, regardless of how you see yourself today, you will find you need to hear the truth I speak.
I chose to forsake my duty. I chose to forsake... these lives that I have overseen for nigh-immemorial eons.
They are your kin, and thus you have the right to know. That is all.
It's been too long.
Are the shadows of that civilization still deep within your memories, Dr. Doctor? Have any fragments floated to the surface?
––Doctor!
It seems like there is at least something there. In any case, there is no point in rehashing the grand visions of the past. Just use your imagination, according to whatever impressions still remain.
But regardless of what you remember, we ended up with far few survivors than you might imagine.
From great desperation was the 'Preserver Project' born. Special life support devices, unshakable underground fortresses. When the calamities receded, they would be the seeds of new life.
But, destruction came, sooner and more overwhelming than anyone could have anticipated. Though at that time, people had already come up with certain methods...
Most of them fail to understand, including she who stands beside you, let alone the blank slate that is you.
We were born out of desperation and not fully prepared. A pure, advanced AI would only increase the risk... so one person made the ultimate sacrifice.
This one volunteered to remain permanently trapped in a cage of consciousness within an electronic cloud, forever locked in an immovable sarcophagus deep underground.

opts: As of this moment... In every sarcophagus, is a person yet to wake up...

lines.x: Correct.
But, none of them will ever wake up again.
They lost that ability long ago.
Even though you have no memory of who you are, you feel a part of your body spasm violently.

A dull pain travels from your heart to the palm of your hand. For a moment, the name of this emotion eludes you, your daily life at Rhodes Island often making you forget the quandary at the core of your existence.
'Kin.'
...Doctor.
Kal'tsit hesitates to speak.
You want her to say something to comfort you. You want her to be as omniscient as she always has been in the past, offering you solutions, answering the questions you've always had.
But, you see only doubt and sorrow in her eyes.
Sorrow is an uncommon emotion for her, even less so if that sorrow is for your sake.
If you came here only to stop Kristen, turn and leave.
Nothing is going to stop us, even if the meaning of her death is itself but a gamble. I've already made up my mind.
The memory-bereft Dr. Doctor, and AMa-10, who has managed to survive to this point, if you have no other responses, then we have nothing more to say.
'No?'
What is it you want then? Do you wish to recover your memories and the truth, or do you insist on stopping Kristen?
You can't accomplish any of it. Your amnesia is hardly so simple. You are just being pushed along numbly, thinking you've acquired faith and new ideals.
Ignorant hope is but the harbinger of despair, something we know so very well. Why not enjoy your hard-fought renewed lease on life, and instead insist on broaching the truth?
No.
We need an answer before disaster strikes. I would even like to have your assistance, if at all possible.
Instead, what you are doing now will only cause things to spin out of control. This land is not capable of coming together as one.
...AMa-10 Kal'tsit.
You cast aspersions on the difficult decision I've made, though I've digested this torment alone for ages.
Do you really know anything about this land?
The threat from the ocean grows day by day, while the mighty Ægirian civilization remains arrogant as ever. They might be powerful now, but they still underestimate the ocean.

Though the Demons and the Hollows deep within in Sami's depths show little tendency to expand, they cannot be ignored. They are like cracks entrenched deep within the ice, presaging a collapse that may occur at any time.
But these are only the fringes beyond civilization's soil. They were never the immediate causes of our slain future.

Inequality and prejudice, war and strife. The people are far from understanding the value of unity, are fundamentally unable to set aside differences and old grudges to talk about peace and the future.
Even when Catastrophes descended, the cogs of Ursus's war machine did not stop turning for a moment, and the peasants of Kazimierz were not exempted from brutal punishment and taxation.
Unity is an eternal dilemma. Even today, there are those who cry for the wars of old, yet refuse to face the suffering as well as those who died in vain. They may not even be able to see these words.
It's too soon... They are still too weak. A few lucid individuals do not represent the entire nation.
Columbia will soon seize their desired advantages from Kristen, expand their appetite, and extend their hypocritical forks and knives towards Bolívar, Sami, and Sargon.
What Kristen did is a far cry from a feat of strength scoping out the future of humanity. Even a slight deviation, and all of this will become but a clarion call for the next round of conquest.
Is that what you, AMa-10, Kal'tsit, think of Terra now?
Fine then... I understand. For one who has walked with the civilizations born of this land, your opinion holds referential value.
Since you each have your own reasons for not wishing to budge, then let us test each other using the ancient ways.
Compared to pure rhetorical persuasion alone, I will need some more 'basal' behaviors to evaluate your quality and revise the plan.
––Provided you are qualified.
(Unknown language) 'Debate.'
Allow me to explain in advance that the things you've brought up are not outside my knowledge. Through Columbia's databases, I have heard of this history.

But, I must re-assess whether the two of you have the qualifications... and the ability to warrant discussing larger plans together. Whether or not Terra can remain in the hands of people like you, whether you have the right to debate the future.
The time Kristen spent here with me goes beyond language. Hold on, you two. We are about to begin.
......Dr.Doctor.
You really don't remember me.
Yes.
This is just her cognitive resonance. Originally, this technology was supposed to be used to check the vital signs of those sleeping in the sarcophagi, or to preserve their dignity.
Just like... entering your dreams, shaping your dreams. For you, I am that dream.

opts: So you're really just an illusion created by that machine?

lines.x: Close enough, but there's still a fundamental difference.
Just as the philosophers and interlocutors did in their palaces of old, when thoughts collide and words are exchanged, cognition intermingles and the boundary between self and non-self blurs.
You currently play the role of the 'Doctor' in your dialogue with 'me'. But in reality, I am the one who plays the 'Doctor,' whereas you are actually 'me.'
But... your consciousness is continuous. This is not equivalent to the illusion in front of you, or your own image changing.
The only word that can describe this sensation is, 'dreamlike.'
You will not see a cold, impassive machine calling itself 'Preserver,' and that will not see the helpless and dazed you. It is only in dreams that our subjective perspectives are no longer distinct.
Come. Don't forget that this manner of cognitive resonance has a humorous name: 'debate.' Oh, of course it is your subconscious mind that finds this word humorous.
Push open that door. There remains a question we must resolve.

opts: Do you really have to test people in their dreams?

lines.x: No, it's not that I'm trying to test you. By all means, this is not one of 'Preserver's' cryptic question-and-answer sessions, or one of Kal'tsit's rhetorical traps.
The one who will pose this question, is Dr. Doctor.

opts: What's that supposed to mean? Myself? Hold on!
But, I've drifted along, never making things hard for myself.

lines.x: You, yourself.

After losing your memories, you quickly got accustomed to life at Rhodes Island. You've always known that Kal'tsit was hiding something from you, but over the years, you've both been overwhelmed by the challenges you face...
It has even caused you to lose your will to pursue the truth. In other words, your curiosity about the truth ended with Babel.
When did you start thinking to yourself, 'this is fine'? Do you really have that same obsession in the bottom of your heart to recover your memories?
As more trust was built, certain truths gradually came to light, which even led you to self-doubt. But, the two of you overcame these difficulties, which is why you're here today.
But, what about before that? Long before you took Amiya's hand in Chernobog, long before Babel.
Who are you? What are you? Where are you going?
Doctor, who are you?
There's something I want to tell you, Doctor.
The Doctor I placed my trust in... is one and the same with the Doctor who woke up in Chernobog and has fought by our side ever since.
That's what I believe. That's what I've always believed.
But in reality? Dr. Kal'tsit... and Theresa... Do they really see the commander of Babel and the Doctor of Rhodes Island as the same person?
I don't know... I just don't know. All I remember is the Doctor who was so kind to me, the Doctor who saved me...
If the truth really is as Dr. Kal'tsit says...
Amiya.
Oh, Dr. Kal'tsit!
The Doctor is tired and needs rest. There is still much to think about and digest.
If there's anything else you'd like to ask, come back tomorrow. Rhodes Island still has a long future ahead of us, so there's no need to rush.
Okay... Sorry, Doctor. I'm sure I caught you off guard by asking this out of the blue.

opts: Actually, I also want to know...

lines.x: Don't dwell on it too much. There's not much time left before we reach the outskirts of Londinium.
The next thing on the itinerary is a meeting between of the elite operators, and we'll need you to sit in.
Hurry, come this way.
Why?
Chernobog has fallen into an atypical circumstance with regard to the Infected.
Rhodes Island, as we are now, cannot afford the risk of getting involved.
The Doctor will be our indispensable linchpin.
Is this one you're talking about worth a dozen or more Rhodes Island lives?
If it's worth it, then I'm on board. But if even a single operator raises a concern, then count me out.
Don't move, Rhodes Island commander!
Hah! Now... Now, there's nothing that white-haired Feline can do! Even if she could kill the captain... even if she could kill Patriot, she can't protect you!
Have you ever thought about our lives? Have you ever looked at this land from our point of view?
Of course not! You don't know a damned thing about the kind of bonds we share, or how we've said goodbye to our parents, or how we've painted our cheeks with the ashes of our best friends!
Killing a Reunion fighter, depriving someone of his life, is just like checking a box for you. Who the hell do you think you are?
Have you ever given a serious thought to what it means for us to live and die?!
Watch out, Doctor!
You're not always the main character of this world.
Just like your life, your past. Take a look outside the glass pane of PRTS. You can never understand everything, or be everywhere at once.
Only those who face the truth deserve to be called 'courageous.'
The ridiculous thing is, you don't have the slightest idea of what the truth is. You've been kept in the dark, you don't understand it, and you're frustrated, even angry, at your own ignorance.
In the end, what more are you than the projection of the pincerbeasts-in-a-bucket around you? They all want to use you for a measure of consolation, to use you for what they want to obtain.
The Reunion members already impaled by black arts point in the direction of those elite operators.
But when you follow their fingertips, you see only a few fowlbeasts flying by.
How many among them faced their own destiny? How many more stood behind Rhodes Island, eventually becoming arrogant overseers?
What about you? You think you can completely sever yourself from the past, just because you've been shaped by new memories? Even if you can, how will you reconcile yourself with the new you?
The onlookers know nothing about you, yet they want to become you, and achieve their vulgar desires through you.
You—
Are you truly worthy of trust? Do you need to be entrusted with such a heavy burden?

opts: I don't plan on abandoning my forgotten past.
I've never tried to escape the responsibilities that came with my past.
But, if the past does not help me fight for Rhodes Island...
If the past has no way of influencing the present...
Then I can only see it as insignificant.

lines.x: This is not a matter you can decide on so cavalierly, Doctor.
Can a criminal be exonerated of his sins just because he lost his memories? If a good person loses her memories, does that erase the good that she has done?
For you in particular... your lost memories are not so... simple.
Perhaps you yourself are not aware of what you have lost. What manner of answer, then, are you able to provide?
Doctor...
...hand!
Take my...
Take my hand!!

opts: ...I will find the meaning within it.
Then, as the 'Doctor,' I will make the correct choice.
It is not the memories of the past that guide me...
...or the events from then to now that have reshaped me.
Just as I always have been.

lines.x: ...I understand your thought process.
Heehee. 'Just as you always have been,' choice words from someone who cannot even recall events from a few years ago.
Even when you come to grasp the truth, be it past or present, it is because you simply 'know' it, not because you 'remember' it, right?
You are a completely new person. But don't worry; you are also the version of yourself closest to your true nature. Because, you've gained a chance to pursue hope once more.
I'm truly curious what the future holds for you, Kal'tsit, and Amiya...
...Sadly, it is not a future that I will be a part of.
Don't worry about it. I'm just doing a little favor.
After all, I'm just a phantasm born from the 'debate' mechanism. Whether I'm real or fake... was never that important to begin with.
But please remember, whether or not it came from the deepest recesses of your consciousness...
'Just as I always have been.' This will become the most important footnote of your life. Only when you truly realize the significance of the words you blurted out–
—Can you live up to those who love you and those you love.
We should all grasp our own destinies; that way, there will no longer be any such thing as preordained destinies in this land.
You are the only antidote, but also the poison that can destroy everything. What truly hinders us is neither Originium nor Catastrophe, neither sea nor sky, but rather ourselves.
Believe in Amiya, believe in Kal'tsit. The dark, endless night is quickly approaching, and you all must become our sole ray of light.
Until next time.
Over here!
Surround her, don't let her through!
Attention all forces! Get ready!
The feeling of weightlessness returns.
Calcium crystals grow rapidly, tethering Saria's hand to the corridor floor.
The second 'Disorder' wave is starting!
...Disorder?
Nasti's magnum opus.
You've been following me around for the last couple of days. Does Defense have nothing else to do?
Sixteen of your Rhine Technological Park modifications do not comply with our established safety regulations.
(Speaks in Sarkaz) Disorder.
You...
Abandon the sense of balance inside your mind. Instead of fighting the chaos, try adapting to its rhythm instead.
Find what you've been looking for?
You reconfigured several key structures in the park. You don't seem to be following any rules, but... the connections between the components make sense.
I could have never imagined that structures like these would be possible.
A linear language constricts most people's imagination.
If you have a specific future in mind, you must first construct a dialogue with the destination, instead of brute-forcing a route.
Abandon... balance.
Perceive the destination...
She frees up both her hands.
The next shift in gravity comes abruptly, and as she hits the ceiling, she takes the opportunity to maneuver her trajectory, and jumps over the soldiers hot on her tail.
Calcium crystals follow in her wake, drawing an arc.
Manipulating raw calcium in the same way microbes change their composition... how very enlightening.
The materials morph extremely quickly, are adaptable and combinable with various organs, and thus have promising medical applications.
This has been your research field ever since you were in college, yes? Why not continue?
Because it's dangerous.
Are you referring to the ethical issues arising from the construction of artificial organs with this technique?
More than that.
Giving new techniques to doctors may save lives; but if they fall into the hands of criminals, they could also be reduced to weapons of destruction.
Haha. I almost forgot how rich with calcium our bodies are.
Therefore, I have determined that my research in this field must be suspended until effective control measures have been established.
It's a terrible pity, you know that?
If you ever change your mind, Rhine Lab could certainly have two sections specializing in life sciences.
Rhine Lab needs a Defense Section more.
I'm giving you my data in the hopes that you can combine our experiences to better evaluate the risks of the chimeric experiments.
Don't go too far, Parvis. The cost of pushing our boundaries will be too high for us all.
Then, where does the boundary lie?
Hm?

Countless studies have shown that the evolution of life has not been a gradual process, contrary to the theories of the earliest scholars in this field. We wouldn't be standing here right now if we had only been taking one step at a time.
Furthermore, if we presume that there is indeed a boundary drawn by a higher will, that determines how we should be acting—
Shouldn't we try to look it in the eye first, before we decide to kneel or raise a flag of rebellion?
...A 'boundary'. This corridor... is also a boundary.
If I keep walking along the corridor... I'll never reach wherever the 'eye' is.
The 'H.A.M.H.R.R.'
This ring-shaped laboratory has from its inception, been designed to subvert people's perceptions.
It is the realization of Kristen's dreams, the real product of Rhine Lab's Components Control Section, the combined wisdom of practically every Rhine Lab Director.
Saria is familiar with how each of them operate, but in the past, she had not allowed herself to think in the same way.
This makes no sense... No, it doesn't matter.
I have long been disgusted with this wall.
The telescope we borrowed from the Stargazers Association broke down.
Don't worry about it. The view here is great, you can see many stars with just the naked eye.
Well, you go look for a nice place to sit down. I'll try fixing it.
Wow... O great superhuman Lady Saria, I'm sooo curious, is there honestly anything you can't do?
I recommend you check my personal course electives. I won't be able to do anything I'm not studying.
You're a terrifying studying machine!
Whoa, it's so cold today... want a candy? Cream puffs? Hot chocolate?
Consuming sweets this late at night is not conducive to good health.
Booooring.
Kristen, look at that rock, what do you think it looks like?
Oh, come on! It looks like Saria, right? It's so hard, and yet it shines so 'perfectly!'
...Like the stars.
Kristen grabs the shining stone, and looks up.
She seems to be comparing it to something, as she moves the rock around.
Something's wrong. There should be a star here, but there isn't one.
I've done so many calculations... and yet I haven't figured out the trajectory of the stars.
You've already failed so many times, you still believe it's measurable?
Hm... I prefer to say that I 'don't believe.'
I don't believe in all the rules I've been taught. I don't believe that humanity will never be able to pierce through the starpod, let alone believe that the stars are only as small as we see them.
There's no way you believe we can fly all the way to the stars, right? I thought only a kid would believe stuff like that!
Children, poets, and scientists.
Haha, who ever said I wasn't one?
Scientists do not believe that dreams are false. Because what they're essentially doing is proving to all humanity that dreams can come true.
Gosh, you really have a one track mind.
So, Kristen. When our Rhine Lab becomes a full fledged public company, are we gonna keep that Lab on the name, or change it to Rhine Something Else?
I'm studying ecology, and Saria's research is in bioengineering and microorganisms, so the two of us are down to keep it nice and generic. Two against one, us in the life sciences... Just kidding.
I mean, you're dead set on soaring through the skies, and your field is high-energy physics, so wouldn't you wanna call it 'Rhine Physics' instead?
We'll stick with 'Rhine Lab.' It's a good name.
I want to know exactly what the stars we're looking at are.
I want to know what's outside the barrier encasing around us.
I want to know... if we really manage to walk among the stars, and look back from over there, what kind of land we've actually been living on.
All the questions I seek have mostly to do with not just me, but 'we.'
And 'we'... we are all beings trapped in this teeny tiny corner.
How are you planning to compensate me for breaking my vivarium, Saria?
You knew Kristen's plans from the very beginning.
Are you talking about 'Arc-01'... or 'Galleria Stellaria'?
'Arc-01' was never more than a front to deceive the military, a pile of scrap paper that was never feasible.
'Galleria Stellaria' is the real laboratory here, or you could also say, the name of this aircraft.
Heh... it certainly is a name that she would come up with.
Yup, the only thing she thinks about.
So you replicated your vivarium up here. Is Kristen intending to break through the barrier with it?
Just tearing the 'starpod' open won't be enough for her, you know.
Without a doubt.
Her fixation was never the barrier, but the realm beyond that even our ancestors have yet to reach.
She truly wants to step inside that deep outer space. She wants to leave everything that can be left behind in that dimension.
And that includes...
Each and every life present in this vivarium.
As soon as I heard about the 'Galleria Stellaria' project, I started gathering a variety of plant species that might be able to survive in the environment outside the barrier.
I just checked, and there's seven hundred and fifty-three different types here. Kristen put them all in this lab.
I'm pretty happy about it... we'll be able to find our answer together real soon.
You want to know if life can exist among the stars.
And especially... yourself.
...Yup.
My people and I... we've been trapped in this cage that just continues to shrink.
As time goes by, we can only follow what happens to water that can't be contained, wind that dissipates, trees that wither, and vanish from this ever changing land.
And then, she said to me...
'Then, let's go.'
'This land is not your home, but beyond it... there might just be a space untainted by Originium that will really accept you and your people.'
A mere hypothesis.

So far, no instrument has managed to penetrate, or even approach the barrier. So there is no basis to assert the possibility of Originium's absence beyond the barrier, let alone the total lack of evidence to suggest that life can survive in the depths of outer space.
I know.
It's just a dream. There's no evidence or data to support it, and it goes against all the scientific knowledge I have.
But... Scientists don't believe that dreams are false. Because what they're essentially doing is proving to all humanity that dreams can come true.
I remember Kristen said that.
Yes. She's the same as ever.
What about us?
Saria...
Have we... changed at all?
Water spreads out like hands in all directions, pinning Saria to the wall.
Saria has never seen water this fierce out of Muelsyse. It is like a waterfall, like a tsunami, as if the water is howling.
Calcium crystals continuously grow from Saria's hands, but they fail to take form under the sweeping waves.
She tries to find Muelsyse, but all she finds in stream after stream of water are the eyes of an elf hidden in the depths of each wave.
Eyes filled with serenity and grief.
The water is howling, but the elf is crying.
I really... really wish, we could be together forever, just like on that starlit night.
But there's no way I can hold onto the both of you at the same time.
Dancing in threes... really is too hard.
Ugh... Ggh...
I won't give you a chance to stop her.
Because... haha, two against one. This time, I'm on her side.
Muel...syse...
Sleep, Saria. Go to sleep.
You're all black and blue and probably about to pass out. There's no need for you to keep fighting, because our dreams are about to come true.
So just close your eyes and the water will take you away from here. By the time you wake up, Kristen and I will have reached our destination.
A solid calcium crystal does not stab at the water in vain, but into the side of the bulkhead.
Saria is well aware that the next 'disorder' is about to happen.
Warning—
Energy overload initiated. All secondary compartmental gravity systems and dampers have failed.
Attention all personnel, energy overload initiated—
Eek?!
Why... would it suddenly...?
Sudden? No.
You were right, Kristen has never changed.
The instantaneous gravitational shift causes the streams of water to lose direction.
The enamel piercing through the bulkhead firmly fixes Saria on the spot, but Muelsyse is tossed outside along with her water.
Saria!
You're the same as ever. Whenever you get nervous, you forget just how fragile your body is.
Uhh... no, catch me! I don't want to leave... not alone...
The energy overload is starting.
When it's done charging, the entire platform will collapse. Even if you can get close to, or even break through the barrier, the core cabin will not last long enough.
Kristen wants to save you.
...As do I.
You...
Yes, I want to bring her back.
Muelsyse... after gravity returns to normal, leave this place.
What happens next is between the two of us.

...Saria!
No! I gotta hold onto something—!
Authentication—failed.
Your clearance has expired. Galleria Stellaria Vivarium—Access Denied.
Your clearance has expired. Galleria Stellaria Vivarium—Access Denied.
Saria! You dummy! Blockhead! Knucklehead! Open the door!
Ha... haha...
I've really been... abandoned again.
Saria... I hope you make it...
Saria stands at the door leading to the deepest part of the lab, knowing her target is just inside.
However, before she can even condense the calcium crystals on her hand, the door lock automatically releases.
Compared to the previous area filled with instruments and equipment, this place feels more like a temple to the stars.
A sphere made of smooth and flawless metal shows off its dazzling beauty under the soft light.
Spheres of varying sizes slowly glide on predetermined orbits one after another, with their paths changing now and then.
The irregularities intertwine with each other, but contrary to expectations, they form an indescribable sense of harmony.
Whether it be the positions of the stars, or how precise their orbits shift, the planetarium exceeds the imaginations of any astrologer.
But in the very next moment, those devoted to the stars would probably fly into a rage.
Those so-called astrologers are basically misguided zealots.
For the past thousand years, they've committed themselves to tracking the trajectory of the stars.
Every trajectory change that occurs signifies a new revelation, or a meaningful omen to them.
Their behavior is not necessarily incomprehensible.
The fact that the stars move without a trace is enough to terrify most people.
To believe that the stars are mere pawns of an undiscoverable being, that their movements and trajectory changes hold any meaning is obviously the more reassuring choice.
However—
Kristen extends a hand toward the planetarium.
The dome of Galleria Stellaria enveloping the ceiling suddenly opens itself.
False starlight floods the laboratory.
Kristen makes a series of gestures with her hand, and in an instant, the planetarium completely transforms.
Fake.
The stars begin to move erratically.
False.
Several stars collide with one another. Though they do not shatter, the sound of them crashing into each other instantly destroys the silence in the room.
Fabricated.
However, Kristen's expression does not change at all.
As if everything is as it should be.
If the trajectories are really just invisible, then how have we avoided colliding with other stars throughout the last millennium?
Are we so special to have avoided such a fate so far?
What kind of land have we actually been living on?
Where exactly are we headed?
These questions... I've whispered them in your ears too many times to count.
Can you help me move that 'planet' over there, Saria?
A small metallic sphere passes by Saria's hand.
'Star.'
Instead of touching it, she lets it fall out of orbit.
What a shame.
I've been fiddling with these planets before we break the barrier, and no matter how many times I try, they just refuse to stay on the right track.
...Stop this, Kristen.
We still have time to turn back, before you hit the barrier with this Galleria Stellaria.
'Galleria Stellaria'... isn't it exactly as how I described it to you before?
A pity that you had already left Rhine Lab by the time I built it.
But I knew you would come back. At the very moment when my dreams were about to be realized... you would be there.
Whether or not you're with me—
—Or against me.
This time, it's my turn to make the first move.
Kristen lifts her hand, revealing the exoskeleton operating system Saria is most familiar with, and gently makes a fist.

Silver liquid spews out from the planetarium behind Kristen, and in an instant, all the planets are thrown out of their orbits, rushing towards a single position at a speed too fast for the naked eye.
The entire galaxy pours down on Saria.
The stars crash to the ground.
Someone lifts a head from the pristine white debris scattered all over the floor.
What's the matter, Control? You were faster before.
Since you weren't able to kill me, I'll launch my counteroffensive.
Don't blink.
The stars struck down by Saria refloat, setting off on a new trajectory.
Kristen's fingers flick to the side.
Saria's legs suddenly feel a thousand times heavier.
The gravity... shifted?
You used this trick to send Muelsyse away.
But it won't work on me.
Enamel grows beneath Saria's feet like a pair of heavy duty boots, holding her in place.
And then, she begins to move forward, step by step.
Instead of refining the way you generate calcium particles, you've simply increased the release speed.
The enamel you're generating has gotten even more brittle.
A hardy resistance... isn't enough to cross the finish line.
A metal planet shatters the enamel barrier, hitting Saria on her shoulder.
Saria does not cease her advance.
It happens twice, and then thrice.
Saria continues to speed up.
Saria, stop.
'Stop'?
That's what I said to you.
I can tell you all about my plans...
I know.
Then why do you insist on stopping me? There will be no other victims in this plan. I just...
Just... wanted to set off some harmless fireworks over the skies of Trimounts.
The moment H.A.M.H.R.R. finishes its job, both the military and Maylander will breathe a sigh of relief.
The residents of Trimounts will be momentarily captivated by the spectacle unfolding before them in the sky, but soon enough their focus will be directed towards other, more deserving concerns.
Rhine Lab... might be the most affected party here. She will lose her Control, possibly several Directors, and the confidence of powerful Columbian organizations.
Then you should have stayed on the ground.
Clean up the mess, protect those who might be hurt by this, and carry our Rhine Lab through the crisis. You've always been good at that.
You forgot again, I resigned... a long time ago.
People like Blake like to call scientists 'mad.'
And we couldn't care any less.
But science is supposed to be rational.
Kristen, when I first heard the story of your parents, I thought... they shouldn't have seen an ending like that.
If they hadn't perished in that accident, and had returned before hitting the barrier...
They would have brought back incomparably precious data, as well as the possibility of a second attempt.
You obviously...
...I admire their will and determination.
But I've never been one to approve of sacrifice, even justified and inevitable.
Ferdinand, Nasti, Parvis... they and many others always thought me too conservative.
They see me as a stickler for rules, obstructing all of you.
Hah... 'Can a woman like this even be called a scientist?'
Even I myself... have asked this question.
You're the most dedicated truth seeker I know.
...The truth.
Truth is the inertia that comes after order. I believe science can bring us truth, and truth can restrain madness, bringing about a better society for everyone.
Us Vouivres have always been accompanied by an instinctive madness, for as long as we can remember. Violence, savagery... we took an extremely long time just to step out of that chaotic darkness.
That's what I have been taught ever since I was small. I use reason to control myself, and desperately... want to confront the madness I see in all of you.
But you can't do it.
Saria, if you intend to hold onto Rhine Lab by your lonesome, you are sure to be disappointed.
Because humanity's thirst for progress is inherently undefeatable.
A type of desire, a type of 'madness,' but also a natural instinct, and the fuel that has brought us from the desolate barrenlands to where we stand.
Which is why I've come here, and not for a fight.
The heavy stars fall on her like a rockslide, and gravity controls the airflow to howl at her entire body.
Calcium crystals crumble from head to toe, but the mountaineer is determined to greet the storm with her own form.
Kristen, I almost admitted my own defeat while searching for you.
Until someone said to me, that I should return to the origin. So on my way here, I've been looking back... at where we all started.
Yes, I dread losing control, but it's not a fear of chaos, but a fear of regression caused by it.
So I must progress onward, as humanity must. Because for us, the trek is far from over.
Indeed, to this day, humanity still lives in the dark.
After all... we can't even see through the sky hanging over our heads.
The road is long—very, very long.
We hold more knowledge than the average bystander, so we must shoulder heavier responsibilities. We should ahead of them, eliminate any dangers, and carefully lead them on the path forward.
A real pioneer should be clearing the way for all humanity, instead of jumping headfirst into the abyss, never to return.
That is my foundation, and I will stand by it.
Caught you.
She has weathered the storm. She crosses the galaxy.
The final calcium crystals spread from one palm to the back of another, and the metal exoskeleton begins to tremble.
The synthetic stars ceaselessly revolve around the two in unison.
Everything else around them has sunk into chaos as a result of their Arts colliding and destroying each other.
Only the protector's will remains unshaken as the earth.
You're always slightly late.
And you're always slightly early. Been waiting long, Kal'tsit?
Not really.
I thought you would be next. You are like a microcosm of everything that happened in Ursus.
I'm sorry, I really am.
Sorry...? For what?
You knew my husband and I were already doomed when we made our decision at the research institute. You're not the type to get depressed...
...No, perhaps you might have even become more depressed in the last century.
No.
I hold regrets, and maybe even remorse towards your story, but not much sorrow.
I'm talking about your daughter. Folinic doesn't live in the better era that you wished for, an era where hate and violence have been forgotten. The same goes for Lyudmila.
Like I said, you're like a microcosm, Lillia.
You and Folinic have an unbreakable bond formed of hatred and blood. A microcosm. I've seen it many a time, a futile destruction.
Sounds like you're tired.
You weren't created to be a savior. You can't level a mountain with a finger, or destroy a city with your eyes.
Haha... just what kind of plebeian would hope that you were invincible? Do those fools think they could uphold a civilization teetering on the edge of collapse with just violence?
There might be some with such a hope.
They hope that I can bring them a future. Hope to survive in my country. Hope for a victory under my command.
You've failed them. Because you're just Kal'tsit.
Our conversation has yielded no answers.
Yes. You've admitted it, you're tired.
But why? You're always on the path of avoiding their self-destruction... but the power you hold is just that miniscule.
There's no way you can teleport from Victoria to Ursus, or monitor every Sarkaz on Terra to see what dangerous witchcraft they're delving into this time.
Whatever you do just grows more and more futile, so you're starting to get impatient, right?
And especially with—
I know.
I know...
Hehe... you already know what you're afraid of, yet you're still able to keep yourself from thinking about it.
Not letting Theresa and Theresis appear in front of you, even your subconsciousness is practically begging for it.
The same goes for your own determination, Kal'tsit, you think people will treat you gently, and so that pair of siblings won't talk to you like this.
How strange. You were so cold before that I never imagined you would seek the warmth of others. From such a perspective, you really have become weak.
Spring is chilly here, I'll catch a cold soon if I stay out any longer. We should go somewhere with a warmer spring.
Shall we go inside?
...Sure.
Snow...?
Hm...? A rare outsider. Are you heading for the sacred mountain?
Pilgrims as young as you rarely make it up here, but why aren't you continuing your ascent? Legs got frostbite?
Sacred mountain... are we in Kjerag?
Kjerag? Where is that?
Is that where you're from?
I wouldn't consider it as such.
It's one of the most spectacular mountains on Terra. I once saw a group of destitute refugees settle and rebuild their lives in that paradise free of Catastrophe.
Oh, it must be a beautiful place then.
Terra... it sounds so small. That's where you're from?
Yes.
...I come from Terra...
And you?
Toward the mountain. We're born with this one goal.
Born with it?
We frolic among the trees on the mossy plains in our youth, and learn from the rocks, and in our prime, we began climbing the mountain.
We did so much groundwork at the foot of the mountain, weaving strands of dawnlight into ropes, or preparing a pair of shoes that won't be contaminated by the darkness of the night.
And then, we begin climbing the mountain.

The road is long and full of obstacles, and we only harvest resources from the valleys. Fortunately for us, the sacred mountain is merciful, and feed us with death, so we dedicate our long lives to this mountain path in return.
It must be an exhausting journey, coming all the way here.
Exhausting? No, no, no.
The snow does not mourn its melting. This is all from the self-flattering efforts of others, while we are just climbers.
What is this sacred mountain called? And what should I call you?
This mountain is called 'time,' and us? We are just insignificant lives.
Time is such a grand thing, isn't it? You should be well aware of this.
Will the mountain change? Has anyone reached the summit?
I don't know. What I mean by that is—maybe.
Try shouting at the abyss of time. The response you'll receive is a gift from the sacred mountain.
Time will not give you an automatic answer, but it will respond. Our predecessors called this response 'fate.'
And fate is the echoes of the cries of life.
Have you never done this before?
Well... it seems like there will be a blizzard tonight.
I should go prepare dinner, and find a place to set up camp... do you need a place to rest? We can go together, girl.
Sure... old man.
I'd like to know, what's there to fill your stomach?
Perhaps we'll be having 'despair,' child.
Maybe you can finish your prayers before dinner. Do your shouting before the blizzard blots everything from sight.
Louder!
I—
You will become yourself.
Kal'tsit. Your judgment is rational. For this I feel sorry.
Sorry for what?
It's only myself...
Because I am your time.
I single-handedly caused your distrust towards humanity, your pessimism, your dismay, and your impatience with no way out.
However... however, from her and on her, you saw a possibility.

Out of your tendency to be overly cautious and anxious, you suspect that this feeling is merely a misjudgment caused by your irritation... after all, the world is breaking out of your knowledge and control. And the new you was only born not long ago.
And yet you've considered it many times before, how much easier it would be, if you chose to trust them instead.
——I am your time. Kal'tsit, you omniscient fool——
You forget the little details, you are not a chosen savior. You should not be. You're not always right either.
Even if you've already made up your mind.
You know better than anyone else how limited time is. And there isn't much left.
You should try giving up. And choose to trust someone you can believe in.
You are not trusted because you are 'Kal'tsit,' nor do you become 'Kal'tsit' because you are trusted.
You are the echoes of a valley, water dripping off a cliff. Your responsibilities weigh beyond what your abilities can bear, and now at least, there are people to share these responsibilities with.
What you have lost and gained in this short period of the past few centuries far exceeds your former days, so long they were that you could only use for progress and evolution.
Everything is about to end, Kal'tsit, and you know this. Will you stay your passivity, your pessimism, your apathy?
Don't forget your original question. Don't forget the answer that you nearly had.
Go on. AMa-10. The meaning of your existence is more than you yourself.
...Let's stop here.
Indeed.
Indeed... this is enough.
And the Doctor?
Calming down elsewhere. I do intend to spend some time with you alone.
I have a question for you, AMa-10, Kal'tsit.
For the past ten thousand years... have you really lived on this land?
Yes.
It seems you've changed quite a lot. We haven't interacted much, but you shouldn't be your current self.
What did you choose?
I've seen wars lasting several millennia, the rise and co-existing of nations, and people growing from their misunderstandings and conflicts.
But in the end, they all scattered to the wind just like the sands of Sargon. But in the blink of an eye, history has already given birth to new children.
I've seen the cycle repeat too many times. The cycle itself looms large and weighs heavy, and I'm fully aware that time is running out.
And then, I ran into a unique opportunity.
I once met two Teekaz, who were closer than any creature in any era, to the truth of continuing civilization.
But now, you are all as fragile as the trash on an asteroid belt, so how can you possibly talk of continuing—
——Wait. You are saying...
The black crown still remains on Terra?
'The Lord of Fiends.' As the ancient Teekaz called it.
Teekaz...? Those odd endemic lifeforms?
What a fluke, I had thought it would have been left behind on...
...The black crown. I dislike the shape it takes on the physical plane, a crown? Leaves a bad taste.
A crown points to royalty. This ancient imagery implies power.
In this downward trend of collapse, where the misunderstandings over ideology and race push surface civilization toward self-destruction, within an extravagant mirage of peace and unity—
The most direct method should be to use those powers to reshape the will of all living beings, and fuse them into a singular form.
What can a false leader made of memories, emotions and knowledge even achieve?
Doctor, are you alright?

opts: This thing has gone too far...

lines.x: Perhaps I have underestimated you, but that does not affect the ending already set in stone.
You want me to believe that such a tiny piece of heritage is enough to change fate?
'Heritage' is humanity's sole achievement ever since our ancient ancestors learned to use tools and fire.
Knowledge, culture, history. So-called civilization is nothing more than that.
There will be a day when the black crown will no longer belong to Amiya alone. And we will have to fight to survive, no matter who the enemy is.
You've chosen to fight for Terra.
This is what you're decided? With this body, this identity? You are different from Kristen and her kind.
Your struggles... are far from your original intent.
Even Rhodes Island's reserve op teams know the difference between fighting for themselves and fighting for others.
And we've all done our preparations, this great land of Terra is where I grew up.
I'm an employee of Rhodes Island.
A part of the ship sailing on the land called Terra.
...'Infected' is just, too trivial a proposition... however... infected... Originium, huh...
Hm...
Then, what about you?
'Doctor'.

opts: I'm an employee of Rhodes Island.
I'm an employee of Rhodes Island...

lines.x: I also have a question for you, Trevor Friston.
How has your time been, in this... equally long period of time?
The 'Perturbation' system has been lifted.
Kristen, tell me how to stop the energy accumulation. And where exactly does the energy come from?
This will be an extremely long story.
I will try to crack the Galleria's control panel before I'm done listening.
Saria, you know, the history of this land is far longer than most realize.
I believe every generation produces its own skeptics, just like my parents and I. Have our ventures really been completely fruitless?
And... was there perhaps a more ancient point in time that the sky hanging over us, was not as we see it today?
I remember. That was one of the establishing goals of Rhine Lab's Scientific Investigation Section.
Conventional exploration alone has already provided me with plenty of interesting information.
After you left Rhine, I picked up some interests I had previously, and tried leaving the laboratory, heading to various corners of the world that mankind had scarcely set foot on.
The purpose of these journeys was not to observe the sky.
The stars are not worth looking at——I came to this conclusion early on, after countless calculations.
I only... wanted to try and find more collaborators.
Since the last Leithanian Arts Detector Orb fell short at 500 meters below the barrier, the academic circles in most countries abandoned their research into the thing.
So what I've been looking for... a voice that doesn't belong to this era.
Like the immortals who rely on Originium Arts to escape the shackles of life, or the Feranmuts older than any country?
Perhaps even older than those regarded as gods, a being so old that history has forgotten them.
Before I set off on my journey, I had no idea what I was going to find. I just... felt a calling. Across time and space, directing me toward that door.
And what... was inside the door?
Everything.
I found the answers to all my questions. How we managed to trek across history to make it here, about the land's unexplored secrets, and about the myriad of possibilities for our future.
But at the same time...
I also saw the end of a civilization, and a spirit abandoned by time.
...Time. Time is nothing but a vile joke.
At the end of the world, when the seas dried up and the rocks rotted away as everything changed, I hid in this grim bunker, repeating a futile act—
—Shouting into the void.
My words permeated every moon and space itself, every tunnel and corner of the stars.
The universe did not respond to me. I prayed to the pitch-black despair for tens of thousands of years, and the only thing I heard in return, was the reverberations of destruction...
Whenever I longed for a survivor's response, the sound of collapse and rays of occupied light still lingering among the stars would emerge...
It was a black hole of futile effort. My emotions were far beyond what any regular lifeform would ever experience or empathize with.
In the endless silence, all I could hear was the heartbeats of my people growing ever weaker, but their dormant hearts should not have been beating at all.
Nightmares cannot jolt me awake, because it is impossible for me to sleep.
I hate myself for having these emotions. I wish I could operate like a pure machine. I hate the other me that assigned me a fate like this.
I knew very well the plan had already concluded. The energy in these... 'sarcophagi' was not enough to power the next stage of recovery in our plans.
Soon after, I was swept into the nothingness. Endless, and numb...
So the contact with Kristen offered you a gamble.
Since your people could never wake up, you might as well...
The first time I saw that humanoid creature, what I felt was... surprise.
I've been watching the depths of space far, far away, not what happened on the surface. I noticed the growth of life, but never expected it to knock on my door.
We spent a long time talking. Though in reality, it was no longer than a few months.
Kristen fired question after question, as if she was meeting a manifestation of truth.
And remarkably, every topic would send her into a long period of reflection and silence, instead of satisfying her thirst for knowledge.
She catalyzed an idea, but she didn't voice it herself—Kristen refused to be the one to execute the plan.

opts: Wasn't she just using Rhine Lab to achieve her goals?

lines.x: She believes that someday, this land will need a group that can separate themselves from politics and unite as a people. This farsighted group would have to employ everything at their disposal...
...To build an ark.
The pioneers would inevitably face the struggle of understanding, and would face division and suffering.
But reality is not myth, as Babel has proven.
So what is Rhodes Island to you, if you hold no hope for all of this?
You're right about that.
We can discuss this so-called 'hope.'
Kristen's little helpers have already begun fiddling with the 'sarcophagi's' technology. I have no qualms with it, but this must mean something important for Terra.
Use it well. Kristen's political views are not without a few flaws.
Loken's research has effectively been put under control. I cannot possibly trust Columbia as a nation, but there is someone behind the Maylander Foundation that is worth negotiating with.
That is good. And then, tell me, are the deep sea-dwelling Ægir really so different from the civilizations on land?
What I can tell you far surpasses whatever Kristen has told you.
Indeed. I cannot comprehend why these creatures with bodily structures inadequately suited to survive in the deep sea would establish a civilization in the ocean.
But this suggests they depend on some product of the seafloor. The form of their civilization has been distorted by external forces, making them stronger, but also causing problems.
Ægir is a settlement that must be united. Regardless of the problems caused by those rampant—'sea monsters'? I believe that's what they are called—Terra cannot leave such a developed nation alone.

Considering their approximate geographical location, and if they use what they might have in abundance appropriately, my guess is that what Kristen is doing right now is not necessarily out of the Ægir's reach.
With strength comes arrogance. Ægir is not so easy to persuade.
Though fortunately, due to a series of incidents, I have had the good fortune to establish a precious friendship with some Ægir.
This will be an impactful breakthrough. Since the Profound Silence, Ægir can no longer remain silent.
Excellent.
The march of the Nightzmora basically only exists as a legend in the databases of Columbia, with no decent historical texts to refer to.
But if the legendary event was not, in fact, a falsehood... then he is indeed an incredible hero.

Even if we take Originium as the power source, the fact that such a tyrant born on Terra thousands of years ago could achieve such a great feat in a much more primitive environment does inspire wonder.
However—the south is already in ruins, so you should look north.
Have you been there?
No. Even further north... at the ends of the endless icefields.
I tried many times, but I've yet to find anything with the limits of my own abilities.
But you know what's over there.
...I do.
So, don't ignore the door that lies there. Push it open when you need to, and it will show you a boundless vista.
Just pushing that door is already far beyond what most are capable of.
And so it is up to all of you to find a way.
The land is vast, and civilization continues to advance, so surely there is more than one Kristen?
You seem to trust the denizens of this land more than I previously thought.
Trust? I hand the tinder to you. I have given up everything on my end.
Whether or not... you can succeed, nothing can be changed... this is my failure. Trust?
I... yes... I do not know.
Perhaps I just want to see any lifeform able to converse and interact with one another, to continue existing.
You know this—compared with the problems on the distant horizon, there is something that could happen at any moment. War.
Even if I look only through the eyes of Columbia, I sense the imminent danger to your world, and the fire that can ignite at any time.
You will require preparation. Do what you must. To extinguish the flame, or to let it produce new life, this is your choice to make.
Those who are truly able to save the denizens of Terra, are always and only themselves.
This need for awakening has not changed since time immemorial, because it is all part of what 'humanity' is.
The sky is about to be opened. But the barrier will soon heal itself. Perhaps it will take a few days, or months, or years.
But the people of Terra will take that limited, veracious scene into their eyes, and will keep the moonlight and the distant planets in their memories.
I hope... Kristen hopes, that this will be a powerful signal.
A signal to herald the next great age.
Saria, would you like to gaze at the stars together? Just like we did before?
We can talk about this after we descend.
...The starry sky.
Or more precisely, what we gaze at is a barrier. The 'starpod.'
There's still a thousand meters to go... our current position is already near the limit of humanity's touch.
The ionization response is too strong. At this height, the instruments can't operate with all the interference.
The control panel is completely useless... I should have known. You have no intention of letting the Galleria Stellaria last much longer.
Yes, it just has to last until I break the barrier.
The barrier... though we have always been inclined towards the usage of 'barrier,' you're well aware that the shape of it is more than just a 'barrier' as per conjecture.
It might not even be a 'barrier' at all, but an extremely wide space.
It might even lack endpoints. This six thousand meters—what we understand as 'normal airspace' is actually 'abnormal.'
And in that case, 'breaking' it was never an option.
It can indeed be broken.
You've never once doubted it.
6972 Meters. If you fly straight up at this angle, as long as you can soar past that height, the Galleria Stellaria will no longer be disturbed by any ionization reactions.
Fine, so you have credible data from that spirit. But this doesn't reduce the danger of exploring the skies at all.
No, even if... if we assume the barrier is enveloping the land like a membrane as you say, it means we know less than nothing about the space beyond it.
This makes your actions even more hazardous. You could even bring disaster upon this land.
I know.
The people are simply inadequately prepared for this.
There will never be 'adequate' preparation. If there is indeed a calamity looming over us, then humanity is already living under a ticking clock.
If that's the case, then we should be even more...
...Even more cautious, right?

Saria, I believe... there will come a day when a much more complete exploration team will be formed, and with more data and full support, they will enter into the depths of space before our very eyes.
This is also the future I hope for.
But you yourself are not in this future of yours.
True.
I'm ill-suited to be called a pioneer. On that point, I fully agree with your view.
If possible... I would prefer to become a starting point.
Just like once, back when... I watched the light of those flames burning in the sky...
That was last thing my parents left me——
——A starting point.
Where's the closest escape pod?
None left. Those with the capability to execute the return module are already gone.
Alright.
Then hold onto me. I will wrap the both of us in calcification. We might not even have a chance of survival, but we can try.
Saria, lower your gaze.
What—
You're the one who didn't look down this time.
The gravity abruptly shifts without warning, and Saria feels only space beneath her feet.
She did not expect Kristen to set up an emergency escape door at the Galleria Stellaria's center.
No, Saria thinks to herself, she should have expected it long before. This door is not for the owner of the planetarium. Kristen knew that at the very last moment, Saria would come.
The sudden change of mass robs Saria of her voice, and she fails to yell out anything at all. She also knows that there is nothing she can grab, but still stretches her hand out.
Starlight and earth pour into her view, while Kristen only gets further and further away.
Saria... you always wanted me to take a good look at the world.
Now I have.
It is... equally beautiful.
You will watch it and the people it nurtures in my stead. This I always knew.
Just as you have in the past, and will in the future.
And I... I'm not the type to lead people on, step by step.
I am just a pair of eyes.
Eyes that... will peer into the unfamiliar depths of space for all that live on that land.
...Friston.
I can almost see the true starry sky... it probably hasn't changed much from what you've told me.
In over ten thousand years...
Me and you, our civilization and yours...
On the scale of the universe, we really are insignificantly small.
A torrent of energy pounds the accumulating area of this giant airborne construct, and the ring hovering up high looks almost swallowed by the tremendous amount of energy.
Immediately afterwards, an even greater amount concentrates at its core.
Enough to destroy any massive nomadic city.
And this world is just so puny and shallow, how could it ever bear it?
The light converges and abandons the earth, stabbing upwards into the air.
Under the unprecedented energy surge, wave after wave of dazzling ripples emerge on the pitch-black curtain devoid of any life.
The false sky is at last rent asunder.
The ancient twin moons expose their real faces.
Tonight, the truth will be perceived by humanity for the very first time.
A coastal view that one can see anywhere in Iberia.
Rubble close by indicates the place may have once been a prosperous village, but now, only a solitary wind chime left hanging on a wall echoes the waves as they hit the shore.
...... (Bewildered slithering sounds)
A wave washes a Sea Terror onto the beach.
No, perhaps it might have followed the wave to dry land.
Whatever it was, it didn't seem to have a goal in mind. It merely wanders, looks around, just like its brethren do.
It shouldn't have found anything. All it can see while standing in this overgrowth is the endless ocean and the sky.
But in the next moment, as if it has sensed something, and looks towards a certain direction.
The never-changing sky is swept by a wave of ripples.
(Doubtful Growling)
It looks in that direction, but there is only an endless sky.
Soon after, its compatriots arrive. Its kin. Its we many.
All their eyes look in the same direction.
As an attendant apologizes to the proprietor, he props up his master—a court musician, and helps him leave the bar.
Mein Herr, you shouldn't drink any more.
Don't... don't get in my way...
I can't... figure out a tune, so I, I might as well just... drink myself to death!
Ach, Mein Herr, didn't you draft several pages yesterday? I think the third song is honestly not bad at all.
Heheh, at least you, you know me well.
But, nein! This will not do! It is not good enough!
There is still... something missing, a key ingredient.
Do you understand? A kind of, psychedelic and yet magnificent, an image of... madness.
Of course, I understand you completely, you're always chasing this kind of work.
Hm? Hmm... Huh?
Oh, I must really be drunk.
What's wrong?
I saw... waves streaking across the horizon.
Waves? I should take you to a doctor... what is that?
The attendant lifts his head towards the sky to see halo-like waves sweep across the night.
This is... could I have somehow gotten second-hand intoxication through the air?
Ha, haha! The clouds are like waves, and the sky is a curtain! Gut, sehr gut!
Theron, go, quick! Take me home!
No, hand me a pen! Quickly!
Here. Have you found some inspiration?
Inspiration? No, what I have discovered is a wellspring!
Under the dim street lights, the musician lies down on the floor on his stomach writing in a frantic pace without a care for his image.
Tonight, many an artist slept nary a wink.
Take a gander at this happy lot.
They think the storm is already over, and Victoria merely had a little hiccup.
Whatever they possess is still intact, and will continue to remain as-is.
How I wish I could share their optimism.
Sir, there's something I need you to see.
What's happened? You're not the sort to be a killjoy at a time like this.
It seems something has happened in Columbia.
Seems?
Yes, we are urgently collecting information at the moment.
Has our rebellious child again done something incomprehensible with the science and technology they're so overly proud of?
That has yet to be determined, but at present, I can confirm that this is information you must know.
Just how important is this matter that I have to be proactive instead of waiting for you to bring it to me?
It is... honestly beyond the scope of my abilities.
The Count realizes the bitter smile on his trusted subordinate's face, and this shifts his originally joking mood to one of surprise.
His officer only knows to tell the truth, and this means that something has indeed happened.
The Count walks to the window, and immediately realized why his subordinate was at a loss for words.
That was information physically impossible to be brought before him, but also a reality that no one could ignore.
What sort of natural phenomenon is this?
We immediately hired experts relevant to this field, and combined with the information from Columbia over the past few days, they have proposed some valuable theories.
...'Site 359 Spy Dossier', 'Investigation Report on the Maylander Foundation'.
And what are these? Academic papers? A barrier...
Tell me the key points of note.
This layer of ripples has originated from Columbia, and is extremely unnatural.
After conducting some simple observations, we believe that the findings align perfectly with a long forgotten theory in the world of science—
The sky above our heads is indeed false.
Something has happened.
And we know nothing about it.
We have already mobilized all our spies in Columbia, and they will soon bring us results.
Spare no expense. I want an answer before tomorrow morning to bring to the Duke of Normandy.
Yes, sir.
My Lord, I would like to introduce my distant nephew to you...
Tonight's banquet ends here and now.
Eh?
The count needs his peace and quiet now, so please return home, Viscount.
V-Very well.
The Count looks at the distant sky without saying a word.
Tonight, conspiracies hatch in alleys while countless politicians toss and turn in their beds.
Warning—
Energy overload. Energy overload. Shockwaves from the energy surplus have impacted the entirety of H.A.M.H.R.R.
Secondary disengagement protocol acceleration complete. All secondary cabins, mounting devices, and external energy lines have been separated from the Galleria Stellaria's main body.
All personnel: evacuate via the escape pod and execute return protocol.
Repeat——All personnel: evacuate via the escape pod and execute return protocol.
This goddamned weightlessness... couldn't they have made the escape pod just a bit smaller?! Not the size of an entire room!
